message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

addedBlocks:
## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  Implementation — mint BURN/BURP regime boilerplate; establish storage
  layout the rest of the heat depends on. Don't preserve existing files.
  
  ## Docket
  Storage layout:
  
  - BURN (project-global, git-tracked):
    `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<viceroyalty>/burn.env`
  - BURP (per-station-user, flat):
    `.buk/rbmu_users/<user>/<investiture>/burp.env`
  
  `.buk/users/` renames to `.buk/rbmu_users/` in this pace.
  
  BURN field reshape:
  
  - Add `BURN_PLATFORM` enum (linux/mac/cygwin/wsl/powershell/localhost).
  - Drop deprecated fields (BURN_TIER, BURN_USER, BURN_SSH_PUBKEY,
    BURN_KEY_FILE, BURN_COMMAND) — content moves to BURP.
  - Final BURN field set: BURN_HOST, BURN_PLATFORM, plus
    workload-template field per paddock.
  
  BURP regime mint (mirror BURN N-instance pattern):
  
  - New `burp_regime.sh`, `burp_cli.sh`, `burp.env` template.
  - Fields: BURP_NODE (cross-references viceroyalty), BURP_USER,
    BURP_SSH_PUBKEY, BURP_KEY_FILE, optional BURP_COMMAND. Derived from
    current BURN's deprecated field list plus the BURP_NODE link.
  - Tabtargets `buw-rp{l,r,v}.{List,Render,Validate}PrivilegeRegime.sh`
    mirror `buw-rn{l,r,v}`.
  - Subdocs BUSTPL/PR/PV mirror BUSTLL/LR/LV (List/Render/Validate
    operation+step+guarantee shape).
  
  BUS0 spec touches:
  
  - Drop deprecated BURN field mapping entries + body anchors.
  - Add BURN_PLATFORM mapping entry + body.
  - Mint BURP regime mapping section + field bodies.
  - Mint `bust_privilege_regime_{list,render,validate}` quoins + bodies.
  - Add three new BUSTPx include lines.
  
  Friendly-error UX: when render/validate is invoked without folio, error
  message lists available instances. Applies to both BURN and BURP.
  
  Existing 7 burn.env files: delete; operator reauthors fresh under new
  layout in `.buk/rbmn_nodes/`.
  
  Done when:
  
  - `tt/buw-rn{l,r,v}` work against new layout (read from `rbmn_nodes/`).
  - `tt/buw-rp{l,r,v}` work against new layout (read from `rbmu_users/`).
  - BURP profile authorable by hand and validates clean.
  - Friendly-error messages land for both regimes.
  - BUS0 validates (asciidoc clean, no orphaned cross-references).
  
  Out of scope: garrison/handbook/ssh-priv (pace 2); BURW boilerplate;
  legacy call-site fixups elsewhere (those break and get cleaned up
  later — heat-wide breakage acknowledged, fixed forward).

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣A-
2	
3	## Goal
4	
5	Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
6	tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and dual Docker daemons.
7	
8	## Current Concept
9	
10	Architectural backbone in BUS0 (`Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc`).
11	Read BUS0 §Remote Node Access first; this paddock captures only state that
12	doesn't fit there.
13	
14	**Three regimes (BURN/BURP/BURW):**
15	
16	- BURN — node-shape (host, OS family, valid workload-template list).
17	  Per-node, git-transported, changes rarely. Identifier: viceroyalty.
18	- BURP — privileged credentials. Per-station-user, operator-authored.
19	  Identifier: investiture. References a viceroyalty via `burp_node`.
20	- BURW — workload state. Per-station-user, system-written by conscript.
21	  Identifier: lieutenancy. References a viceroyalty via `burw_node`.
22	
23	**Cross-cutting premises:**

26	  reach it by reference, never duplicate.
27	- `bus_keys_operator_owned` — system never generates or modifies SSH key
28	  material; operator owns all key administration.
29	- single-admin-at-a-time — one privileged admin per node at a time;
30	  sequential transitions, not concurrent residence.
31	
32	**Verb vocabulary:**
33

35	  vanquish, inventory
36	- Workload tier: knock, remote_run, interactive_session
37	
38	**Tabtarget colophons under `buw-`:**
39	
40	- `buw-rn{l,r,v}` — BURN regime ops; analogous `buw-rp/rw{l,r,v}` for BURP/BURW
41	- `buw-np*` — privileged-tier verbs (np = uses privileged authority)
42	- `buw-nw*` — workload-tier verbs (nw = uses workload identity)
43	- `buw-hn0` — handbook landing for node ops
44

46	
47	- `BURS_KEY_DIR` — operator-managed SSH key directory (full path, no tilde)

# jjezs_paddock ₣A-

## Goal

Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Run RB's container isolation
tests on a Windows host with WSL, Cygwin, and dual Docker daemons.

## Current Concept

Architectural backbone in BUS0 (`Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUS0-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc`).
Read BUS0 §Remote Node Access first; this paddock captures only state that
doesn't fit there.

**Three regimes (BURN/BURP/BURW):**

- BURN — node-shape (host, OS family, valid workload-template list).
  Per-node, git-transported, changes rarely. Identifier: viceroyalty.
- BURP — privileged credentials. Per-station-user, operator-authored.
  Identifier: investiture. References a viceroyalty via `burp_node`.
- BURW — workload state. Per-station-user, system-written by conscript.
  Identifier: lieutenancy. References a viceroyalty via `burw_node`.

**Cross-cutting premises:**

- `burn_host_singularity` — host of a node lives only in BURN; BURP and BURW
  reach it by reference, never duplicate.
- `bus_keys_operator_owned` — system never generates or modifies SSH key
  material; operator owns all key administration.
- single-admin-at-a-time — one privileged admin per node at a time;
  sequential transitions, not concurrent residence.

**Verb vocabulary:**

- Privileged tier: garrison, withdraw, conscript-{platform}, discharge,
  vanquish, inventory
- Workload tier: knock, remote_run, interactive_session

**Tabtarget colophons under `buw-`:**

- `buw-rn{l,r,v}` — BURN regime ops; analogous `buw-rp/rw{l,r,v}` for BURP/BURW
- `buw-np*` — privileged-tier verbs (np = uses privileged authority)
- `buw-nw*` — workload-tier verbs (nw = uses workload identity)
- `buw-hn0` — handbook landing for node ops

**BURS additions:**

- `BURS_KEY_DIR` — operator-managed SSH key directory (full path, no tilde)
- `BURS_WORKLOAD_STATE_DIR` — where BURW state files live (per-station-user)

## Heat Sequence

**Spec finalization (gates implementation):**

- reshape-spec-finalize — finish BUS0 verb table, cross-cutting principles
  section, subdoc consolidation

**Cleanup (depends on spec finalize):**

- AAf (scope-expanded) — delete deprecated BURN fields, residue prose,
  retired subdocs, legacy buhw handbook tabtargets, legacy
  `buw-rhc{l,m,x}` profile-minting if confirmed unused

**Investigation (independent, runs anytime):**

- AAe — localhost-fundus-parallel-saturation diagnosis

**Practice phase (Windows host) — blocked on yet-to-mint implementation paces:**

- AAD — WSL distro + Cygwin install
- AAE — JJK fundus inside WSL
- AAF — Docker dual-daemon

**Implementation paces:** TBD. Minted fresh after spec finalize + cleanup land.
Will cover garrison, withdraw, conscript-{platform}, discharge, vanquish,
BURS field additions, BURP/BURW regime tabtargets.

## Deferred (named for future return)

- **Adopt** — cross-station-user pubkey install via existing privileged trust.
  Today: each station user runs the ceremony fresh from their own machine.
- **User regime** — centralized per-user pubkey for shared admin scenarios.
  Pairs naturally with Adopt when both are needed.
- **Git normalization on remote** — JJK has `jjx_plant`; BUK doesn't duplicate.

## Standing Notes

- Tailscale provides stable transport (Mac sees `rocket` as
  `100.x.y.z` / tailnet hostname); not adopted as a BUK dependency
- Windows username `b hyslop` (with space) — audit shell quoting at
  every `${BURP_USER}` expansion during practice
- Fundus capability registry: `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSFR-FundusRegistry.md`
- PowerShell-from-WSL pattern: bash owns control flow, PowerShell is the
  Windows syscall layer; one atomic command per call
- BURS_USER station regime field routes BURP files to
  `.buk/users/${BURS_USER}/` subdirectory; BURN files are shared
  (project-tracked); BURW state lives at `${BURS_WORKLOAD_STATE_DIR}`

# jjezs_pace ₢A-AAj

## Character
Implementation — mint BURN/BURP regime boilerplate; establish storage
layout the rest of the heat depends on. Don't preserve existing files.

## Docket
Storage layout:

- BURN (project-global, git-tracked):
  `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<viceroyalty>/burn.env`
- BURP (per-station-user, flat):
  `.buk/rbmu_users/<user>/<investiture>/burp.env`

`.buk/users/` renames to `.buk/rbmu_users/` in this pace.

BURN field reshape:

- Add `BURN_PLATFORM` enum (linux/mac/cygwin/wsl/powershell/localhost).
- Drop deprecated fields (BURN_TIER, BURN_USER, BURN_SSH_PUBKEY,
  BURN_KEY_FILE, BURN_COMMAND) — content moves to BURP.
- Final BURN field set: BURN_HOST, BURN_PLATFORM, plus
  workload-template field per paddock.

BURP regime mint (mirror BURN N-instance pattern):

- New `burp_regime.sh`, `burp_cli.sh`, `burp.env` template.
- Fields: BURP_NODE (cross-references viceroyalty), BURP_USER,
  BURP_SSH_PUBKEY, BURP_KEY_FILE, optional BURP_COMMAND. Derived from
  current BURN's deprecated field list plus the BURP_NODE link.
- Tabtargets `buw-rp{l,r,v}.{List,Render,Validate}PrivilegeRegime.sh`
  mirror `buw-rn{l,r,v}`.
- Subdocs BUSTPL/PR/PV mirror BUSTLL/LR/LV (List/Render/Validate
  operation+step+guarantee shape).

BUS0 spec touches:

- Drop deprecated BURN field mapping entries + body anchors.
- Add BURN_PLATFORM mapping entry + body.
- Mint BURP regime mapping section + field bodies.
- Mint `bust_privilege_regime_{list,render,validate}` quoins + bodies.
- Add three new BUSTPx include lines.

Friendly-error UX: when render/validate is invoked without folio, error
message lists available instances. Applies to both BURN and BURP.

Existing 7 burn.env files: delete; operator reauthors fresh under new
layout in `.buk/rbmn_nodes/`.

Done when:

- `tt/buw-rn{l,r,v}` work against new layout (read from `rbmn_nodes/`).
- `tt/buw-rp{l,r,v}` work against new layout (read from `rbmu_users/`).
- BURP profile authorable by hand and validates clean.
- Friendly-error messages land for both regimes.
- BUS0 validates (asciidoc clean, no orphaned cross-references).

Out of scope: garrison/handbook/ssh-priv (pace 2); BURW boilerplate;
legacy call-site fixups elsewhere (those break and get cleaned up
later — heat-wide breakage acknowledged, fixed forward).

content.text:
Mounted ₣A- — `rbk-mvp-3-draft-windows-procedures` (11/20 done, racing).

**Paddock summary:** Windows test infrastructure for Recipe Bottle. Three-regime architecture (BURN node-shape, BURP privileged-creds, BURW workload-state) with BUS0 as the architectural backbone. Verb vocabulary (garrison/withdraw/conscript/discharge/vanquish/inventory + knock/remote_run/interactive_session) and tabtarget colophons (`buw-rn/rp/rw{l,r,v}`, `buw-np*`, `buw-nw*`) defined. Implementation paces minted after spec finalize + cleanup.

**Recent work (last 3 commits affiliated with the heat):**
- d (3c) — node-tabtarget-subdocs
- V (5c) — burh-to-burn-rename in buk
- W (2c) — burh-to-burn-rename in jjk

**Current pace:** ₢A-AAj `mint-burn-burp-boilerplate` [rough] — establish storage layout the rest of the heat depends on.

**Docket asks for:**
1. **Storage layout**: rename `.buk/users/` → `.buk/rbmu_users/`; new `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<viceroyalty>/burn.env` and `.buk/rbmu_users/<user>/<investiture>/burp.env`
2. **BURN field reshape**: add `BURN_PLATFORM` enum, drop 5 deprecated fields (TIER/USER/SSH_PUBKEY/KEY_FILE/COMMAND), final set is HOST + PLATFORM + workload-template
3. **BURP regime mint**: new `burp_regime.sh`, `burp_cli.sh`, `burp.env` template; tabtargets `buw-rp{l,r,v}`; subdocs BUSTPL/PR/PV mirroring BUSTLL/LR/LV
4. **BUS0 spec touches**: drop deprecated mapping entries, add BURN_PLATFORM, mint BURP regime + privilege quoins + 3 BUSTPx includes
5. **Friendly-error UX** for missing folio (both regimes)
6. **Delete the 7 existing burn.env files** — operator reauthors

**Name assessment:** `mint-burn-burp-boilerplate` fits the docket — it's exactly the establish-the-skeleton pace.

**Proposed approach (4 bullets):**
- Land BUS0 spec changes first (drop deprecated mappings, add BURN_PLATFORM, mint BURP mapping section + privilege quoins, mint BUSTPx subdocs) — spec drives implementation
- Reshape `burn_regime.sh`/`burn_cli.sh` to new field set + new path `.buk/rbmn_nodes/`; add BURN_PLATFORM enum validation; add friendly-error on missing folio
- Mint BURP boilerplate by mirror — `burp_regime.sh`, `burp_cli.sh`, `burp.env` template, three `buw-rp*` tabtargets; route to `.buk/rbmu_users/<user>/<investiture>/`
- Delete the 7 existing burn.env files + rename `.buk/users/` → `.buk/rbmu_users/` (operator reauthors)

**Execution strategy:** Sequential opus — this is intricate spec-and-mirror work touching three coordinated layers (spec, regime modules, tabtargets) where the BURN reshape and BURP mint must stay in lockstep with BUS0; mistakes propagate. Not parallelizable — order matters (spec → BURN reshape → BURP mirror → cleanup).

Proceed?

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BURN Regime - Bash Utility Regime Node Module
20	#
21	# BURN is a multi-instance regime — one file per SSH connection profile.
22	# Each profile lives at .buk/users/${BURS_USER}/<alias>/burn.env
23	
24	set -euo pipefail
25	
26	# Multiple inclusion detection
27	test -z "${ZBURN_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module burn multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
28	ZBURN_SOURCED=1
29	
30	######################################################################
31	# Internal Functions (zburn_*)
32	
33	zburn_kindle() {
34	  test -z "${ZBURN_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burn already kindled"
35	
36	  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
37	  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.
38	
39	  # Enroll all BURN variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering
40	
41	  buv_regime_enroll BURN
42	
43	  buv_group_enroll "Connection Identity"
44	  buv_string_enroll  BURN_HOST        1  253  "IP address or hostname of the remote machine"
45	  buv_xname_enroll   BURN_USER        1   32  "Username on the remote host"
46	  buv_xname_enroll   BURN_ALIAS       1   64  "SSH alias and key filename (must match directory name)"
47	
48	  buv_group_enroll "Authentication"
49	  buv_string_enroll  BURN_SSH_PUBKEY  20 1024  "Full public key line (e.g., ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host)"
50	  buv_string_enroll  BURN_KEY_FILE     0  256  "SSH private key filename in ~/.ssh/ (empty defaults to alias)"
51	
52	  buv_group_enroll "Session Routing"
53	  buv_string_enroll  BURN_COMMAND      0  512  "Shell command for command= routing in authorized_keys (empty for direct shell)"
54	
55	  buv_group_enroll "Privilege Tier"
56	  buv_enum_enroll    BURN_TIER  "Tier classification: privileged for node-level ops, workload for ephemeral remote work"  privileged  workload
57	
58	  # Guard against unexpected BURN_ variables not in enrollment
59	  buv_scope_sentinel BURN BURN_
60	
61	  # Lock all enrolled BURN_ variables against mutation
62	  buv_lock BURN
63	
64	  readonly ZBURN_KINDLED=1
65	}
66	
67	zburn_sentinel() {
68	  test "${ZBURN_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burn not kindled - call zburn_kindle first"
69	}
70	
71	# Enforce all BURN enrollment validations
72	zburn_enforce() {
73	  zburn_sentinel
74	
75	  buv_vet BURN
76	}
77	
78	######################################################################
79	# Public Functions (burn_*)
80	
81	# Build a BURN authorized_keys line with alias marker.
82	# Pure string builder — no I/O. Caller decides how to write.
83	# Sets ZBURN_KEY_LINE (the full line) and ZBURN_KEY_MARKER (the grep tag).
84	# Prerequisite: BURN kindled (needs BURN_ALIAS, BURN_COMMAND, BURN_SSH_PUBKEY)
85	zburn_build_key_line() {
86	  zburn_sentinel
87	
88	  ZBURN_KEY_MARKER="# BURN:${BURN_ALIAS}"
89	
90	  if test -n "${BURN_COMMAND}"; then
91	    ZBURN_KEY_LINE="command=\"${BURN_COMMAND}\" ${BURN_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURN_KEY_MARKER}"
92	  else
93	    ZBURN_KEY_LINE="${BURN_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURN_KEY_MARKER}"
94	  fi
95	}
96	
97	# List available alias names as space-separated tokens.
98	# Emits one <alias>.${BUF_EXT_ALIAS} observation fact per profile (empty
99	# content; presence is the fact).
100	# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (needs BURS_USER), BURD kindled (needs BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
101	burn_list_capture() {
102	  zburs_sentinel
103	
104	  local -r z_user_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}"
105	  test -d "${z_user_dir}" || return 1
106	
107	  local z_result=""
108	  local z_files=("${z_user_dir}"/*/burn.env)
109	  local z_i=""
110	  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
111	    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
112	    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
113	    local z_alias="${z_dir##*/}"
114	    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_alias}" "${BUF_EXT_ALIAS}" ""
115	    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_alias}"
116	  done
117	  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
118	  echo "${z_result}"
119	}
120	
121	# Assert that the named alias's profile has the expected BURN_TIER value.
122	# Exits non-zero with diagnostic before any side effect on tier mismatch.
123	# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (BURS_USER), BURD kindled (BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
124	burn_assert_tier() {
125	  zburs_sentinel
126	
127	  local -r z_expected="${1:-}"
128	  local -r z_alias="${2:-}"
129	  test -n "${z_expected}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: expected tier required (privileged|workload)"
130	  test -n "${z_alias}"    || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: alias required"
131	
132	  local -r z_profile_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${z_alias}/burn.env"
133	  test -f "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: profile not found for alias '${z_alias}': ${z_profile_file}"
134	
135	  local z_observed=""
136	  local z_line=""
137	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
138	    case "${z_line}" in
139	      BURN_TIER=*) z_observed="${z_line#BURN_TIER=}" ; break ;;
140	    esac
141	  done < "${z_profile_file}"
142	
143	  test -n "${z_observed}"                || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: BURN_TIER missing or empty in profile for alias '${z_alias}'"
144	  test "${z_observed}" = "${z_expected}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: alias '${z_alias}' has BURN_TIER='${z_observed}', expected '${z_expected}'"
145	}
146	
147	# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURN Regime - Bash Utility Regime Node Module
#
# BURN is a multi-instance regime — one file per SSH connection profile.
# Each profile lives at .buk/users/${BURS_USER}/<alias>/burn.env

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBURN_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module burn multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBURN_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zburn_*)

zburn_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBURN_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burn already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  # Enroll all BURN variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll BURN

  buv_group_enroll "Connection Identity"
  buv_string_enroll  BURN_HOST        1  253  "IP address or hostname of the remote machine"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURN_USER        1   32  "Username on the remote host"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURN_ALIAS       1   64  "SSH alias and key filename (must match directory name)"

  buv_group_enroll "Authentication"
  buv_string_enroll  BURN_SSH_PUBKEY  20 1024  "Full public key line (e.g., ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host)"
  buv_string_enroll  BURN_KEY_FILE     0  256  "SSH private key filename in ~/.ssh/ (empty defaults to alias)"

  buv_group_enroll "Session Routing"
  buv_string_enroll  BURN_COMMAND      0  512  "Shell command for command= routing in authorized_keys (empty for direct shell)"

  buv_group_enroll "Privilege Tier"
  buv_enum_enroll    BURN_TIER  "Tier classification: privileged for node-level ops, workload for ephemeral remote work"  privileged  workload

  # Guard against unexpected BURN_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel BURN BURN_

  # Lock all enrolled BURN_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock BURN

  readonly ZBURN_KINDLED=1
}

zburn_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURN_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burn not kindled - call zburn_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all BURN enrollment validations
zburn_enforce() {
  zburn_sentinel

  buv_vet BURN
}

######################################################################
# Public Functions (burn_*)

# Build a BURN authorized_keys line with alias marker.
# Pure string builder — no I/O. Caller decides how to write.
# Sets ZBURN_KEY_LINE (the full line) and ZBURN_KEY_MARKER (the grep tag).
# Prerequisite: BURN kindled (needs BURN_ALIAS, BURN_COMMAND, BURN_SSH_PUBKEY)
zburn_build_key_line() {
  zburn_sentinel

  ZBURN_KEY_MARKER="# BURN:${BURN_ALIAS}"

  if test -n "${BURN_COMMAND}"; then
    ZBURN_KEY_LINE="command=\"${BURN_COMMAND}\" ${BURN_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURN_KEY_MARKER}"
  else
    ZBURN_KEY_LINE="${BURN_SSH_PUBKEY} ${ZBURN_KEY_MARKER}"
  fi
}

# List available alias names as space-separated tokens.
# Emits one <alias>.${BUF_EXT_ALIAS} observation fact per profile (empty
# content; presence is the fact).
# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (needs BURS_USER), BURD kindled (needs BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
burn_list_capture() {
  zburs_sentinel

  local -r z_user_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}"
  test -d "${z_user_dir}" || return 1

  local z_result=""
  local z_files=("${z_user_dir}"/*/burn.env)
  local z_i=""
  for z_i in "${!z_files[@]}"; do
    test -f "${z_files[$z_i]}" || continue
    local z_dir="${z_files[$z_i]%/*}"
    local z_alias="${z_dir##*/}"
    buf_write_fact_multi "${z_alias}" "${BUF_EXT_ALIAS}" ""
    z_result="${z_result}${z_result:+ }${z_alias}"
  done
  test -n "${z_result}" || return 1
  echo "${z_result}"
}

# Assert that the named alias's profile has the expected BURN_TIER value.
# Exits non-zero with diagnostic before any side effect on tier mismatch.
# Prerequisite: BURS kindled (BURS_USER), BURD kindled (BURD_CONFIG_DIR)
burn_assert_tier() {
  zburs_sentinel

  local -r z_expected="${1:-}"
  local -r z_alias="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_expected}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: expected tier required (privileged|workload)"
  test -n "${z_alias}"    || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: alias required"

  local -r z_profile_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${z_alias}/burn.env"
  test -f "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: profile not found for alias '${z_alias}': ${z_profile_file}"

  local z_observed=""
  local z_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    case "${z_line}" in
      BURN_TIER=*) z_observed="${z_line#BURN_TIER=}" ; break ;;
    esac
  done < "${z_profile_file}"

  test -n "${z_observed}"                || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: BURN_TIER missing or empty in profile for alias '${z_alias}'"
  test "${z_observed}" = "${z_expected}" || buc_die "burn_assert_tier: alias '${z_alias}' has BURN_TIER='${z_observed}', expected '${z_expected}'"
}

# eof

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# BURN CLI - Command line interface for BURN node regime operations
20	#
21	# Four command families:
22	#   burn_validate/render/list  — regime read operations (require BUZ_FOLIO)
23	#   burn_construct_*           — profile constructors (write burn.env)
24	#   burn_verify_*              — SSH automation (verify)
25	#   burn_install_key           — authorized_keys management (per-profile, idempotent)
26	
27	set -euo pipefail
28	
29	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
30	
31	######################################################################
32	# Command Functions — Regime Operations
33	
34	burn_validate() {
35	  buc_doc_brief "Validate BURN node regime profile via enrollment report"
36	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
37	
38	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
39	  buc_step "Validating BURN node regime"
40	  buv_report BURN "Node Regime"
41	  buc_step "BURN node regime valid"
42	}
43	
44	burn_render() {
45	  buc_doc_brief "Display diagnostic view of BURN node regime profile"
46	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
47	
48	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
49	  buv_render BURN "BURN - Bash Utility Node Regime"
50	}
51	
52	burn_list() {
53	  buc_doc_brief "List available BURN host profiles for current user"
54	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
55	
56	  local z_aliases
57	  z_aliases=$(burn_list_capture) || buc_die "No BURN profiles found for user: ${BURS_USER}"
58	  buc_step "Available profiles for ${BURS_USER}:"
59	  local z_alias=""
60	  for z_alias in ${z_aliases}; do
61	    buc_bare "        ${z_alias}"
62	  done
63	}
64	
65	######################################################################
66	# Internal: Constructor Helper
67	#
68	# Shared logic for all platform constructors.
69	# Args: host user moniker suffix command_value key_file
70	# command_value and key_file may be empty.
71	
72	zburn_construct() {
73	  local z_host="${1:-}"
74	  local z_user="${2:-}"
75	  local z_moniker="${3:-}"
76	  local z_suffix="${4:-}"
77	  local z_command="${5:-}"
78	  local z_key_file="${6:-}"
79	
80	  test -n "${z_host}"    || buc_die "host required (arg 1)"
81	  test -n "${z_user}"    || buc_die "user required (arg 2)"
82	  test -n "${z_moniker}" || buc_die "moniker required (arg 3)"
83	
84	  local -r z_alias="${z_moniker}-${z_suffix}"
85	  local -r z_profile_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${z_alias}"
86	  local -r z_profile_file="${z_profile_dir}/burn.env"
87	  local -r z_pubkey_file="${HOME}/.ssh/${z_alias}.pub"
88	  local z_pubkey=""
89	  local z_has_pubkey=0
90	
91	  if test -f "${z_pubkey_file}"; then
92	    z_pubkey=$(head -1 "${z_pubkey_file}")
93	    z_has_pubkey=1
94	    buc_step "Found public key: ${z_pubkey_file}"
95	  else
96	    z_pubkey="ssh-ed25519 REPLACE_WITH_ACTUAL_PUBKEY ${BURS_USER}@$(hostname -s)"
97	  fi
98	
99	  mkdir -p "${z_profile_dir}"
100	
101	  printf '%s\n' "BURN_HOST=${z_host}"           >  "${z_profile_file}"
102	  printf '%s\n' "BURN_USER=${z_user}"            >> "${z_profile_file}"
103	  printf '%s\n' "BURN_ALIAS=${z_alias}"          >> "${z_profile_file}"
104	  printf '%s\n' "BURN_SSH_PUBKEY='${z_pubkey}'"  >> "${z_profile_file}"
105	  printf '%s\n' "BURN_KEY_FILE=${z_key_file}"    >> "${z_profile_file}"
106	  printf '%s\n' "BURN_COMMAND='${z_command}'"    >> "${z_profile_file}"
107	
108	  buc_step "BURN profile written: ${z_profile_file}"
109	
110	  if test "${z_has_pubkey}" = "0"; then
111	    buh_e
112	    buh_section "SSH Key Required"
113	    buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_pubkey_file}"; local -r z_pubkey_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
114	    buh_line "No public key at ${z_pubkey_yelp}."
115	    buh_line   "Generate one:"
116	    buh_e
117	    buh_code   "ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/${z_alias}"
118	    buh_e
119	    buh_line   "Then re-run this constructor to populate the public key."
120	  fi
121	}
122	
123	######################################################################
124	# Command Functions — Constructors
125	
126	burn_construct_linux() {
127	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURN profile for Linux target"
128	  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Linux machine"
129	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the remote host"
130	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-linux)"
131	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
132	
133	  zburn_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "linux" ""
134	}
135	
136	burn_construct_mac() {
137	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURN profile for macOS target"
138	  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Mac"
139	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the remote host"
140	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-mac)"
141	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
142	
143	  zburn_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "mac" ""
144	}
145	
146	burn_construct_localhost() {
147	  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURN profile for localhost (local SSH)"
148	  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on this machine"
149	  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-local)"
150	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
151	
152	  zburn_construct "localhost" "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "local" "" "id_ed25519"
153	}
154	
155	######################################################################
156	# Command Functions — SSH Automation
157	
158	burn_verify_ssh() {
159	  buc_doc_brief "Verify SSH connectivity to a BURN profile"
160	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
161	
162	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
163	
164	  buc_step "Testing SSH to ${BURN_ALIAS} (${BURN_HOST})..."
165	  local z_result=""
166	  if z_result=$(ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=10 "${BURN_ALIAS}" whoami 2>&1); then
167	    buc_success "SSH OK: ${BURN_ALIAS} -> ${z_result}"
168	  else
169	    buc_die "SSH FAILED: ${BURN_ALIAS} -> ${z_result}"
170	  fi
171	}
172	
173	burn_install_key() {
174	  buc_doc_brief "Install BURN profile SSH key into authorized_keys (idempotent)"
175	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
176	
177	  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
178	
179	  local -r z_auth_keys="${HOME}/.ssh/authorized_keys"
180	
181	  zburn_build_key_line
182	
183	  mkdir -p "${HOME}/.ssh"
184	
185	  if test -f "${z_auth_keys}"; then
186	    local -r z_temp=$(mktemp)
187	    grep -v "${ZBURN_KEY_MARKER}" "${z_auth_keys}" > "${z_temp}" || true
188	    mv "${z_temp}" "${z_auth_keys}"
189	  fi
190	
191	  printf '%s\n' "${ZBURN_KEY_LINE}" >> "${z_auth_keys}"
192	  chmod 600 "${z_auth_keys}"
193	
194	  buc_success "Key installed for ${BURN_ALIAS} in ${z_auth_keys}"
195	}
196	
197	######################################################################
198	# Furnish and Main
199	
200	zburn_furnish() {
201	  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
202	  buc_doc_env "BURD_CONFIG_DIR       " "BUK config directory (dispatch-provided)"
203	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
204	
205	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
206	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
207	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burc_regime.sh"
208	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burs_regime.sh"
209	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
210	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burn_regime.sh"
211	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bupr_PresentationRegime.sh"
212	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
213	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"
214	
215	  zbuv_kindle
216	  zburd_kindle
217	  zburd_enforce
218	
219	  source "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURC: ${BURD_REGIME_FILE}"
220	
221	  zburc_kindle
222	  zburc_enforce
223	
224	  source "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURS: ${BURD_STATION_FILE}"
225	
226	  zburs_kindle
227	  zburs_enforce
228	
229	  zbupr_kindle
230	
231	  # If BUZ_FOLIO is set, load and kindle the specified profile
232	  if test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"; then
233	    local -r z_profile_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/burn.env"
234	    test -f "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "BURN profile not found: ${z_profile_file}"
235	    source "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURN: ${z_profile_file}"
236	    zburn_kindle
237	    zburn_enforce
238	  fi
239	}
240	
241	buc_execute burn_ "Bash Utility Node Regime" zburn_furnish "$@"
242	
243	# eof
244

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURN CLI - Command line interface for BURN node regime operations
#
# Four command families:
#   burn_validate/render/list  — regime read operations (require BUZ_FOLIO)
#   burn_construct_*           — profile constructors (write burn.env)
#   burn_verify_*              — SSH automation (verify)
#   burn_install_key           — authorized_keys management (per-profile, idempotent)

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

######################################################################
# Command Functions — Regime Operations

burn_validate() {
  buc_doc_brief "Validate BURN node regime profile via enrollment report"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
  buc_step "Validating BURN node regime"
  buv_report BURN "Node Regime"
  buc_step "BURN node regime valid"
}

burn_render() {
  buc_doc_brief "Display diagnostic view of BURN node regime profile"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"
  buv_render BURN "BURN - Bash Utility Node Regime"
}

burn_list() {
  buc_doc_brief "List available BURN host profiles for current user"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local z_aliases
  z_aliases=$(burn_list_capture) || buc_die "No BURN profiles found for user: ${BURS_USER}"
  buc_step "Available profiles for ${BURS_USER}:"
  local z_alias=""
  for z_alias in ${z_aliases}; do
    buc_bare "        ${z_alias}"
  done
}

######################################################################
# Internal: Constructor Helper
#
# Shared logic for all platform constructors.
# Args: host user moniker suffix command_value key_file
# command_value and key_file may be empty.

zburn_construct() {
  local z_host="${1:-}"
  local z_user="${2:-}"
  local z_moniker="${3:-}"
  local z_suffix="${4:-}"
  local z_command="${5:-}"
  local z_key_file="${6:-}"

  test -n "${z_host}"    || buc_die "host required (arg 1)"
  test -n "${z_user}"    || buc_die "user required (arg 2)"
  test -n "${z_moniker}" || buc_die "moniker required (arg 3)"

  local -r z_alias="${z_moniker}-${z_suffix}"
  local -r z_profile_dir="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${z_alias}"
  local -r z_profile_file="${z_profile_dir}/burn.env"
  local -r z_pubkey_file="${HOME}/.ssh/${z_alias}.pub"
  local z_pubkey=""
  local z_has_pubkey=0

  if test -f "${z_pubkey_file}"; then
    z_pubkey=$(head -1 "${z_pubkey_file}")
    z_has_pubkey=1
    buc_step "Found public key: ${z_pubkey_file}"
  else
    z_pubkey="ssh-ed25519 REPLACE_WITH_ACTUAL_PUBKEY ${BURS_USER}@$(hostname -s)"
  fi

  mkdir -p "${z_profile_dir}"

  printf '%s\n' "BURN_HOST=${z_host}"           >  "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURN_USER=${z_user}"            >> "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURN_ALIAS=${z_alias}"          >> "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURN_SSH_PUBKEY='${z_pubkey}'"  >> "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURN_KEY_FILE=${z_key_file}"    >> "${z_profile_file}"
  printf '%s\n' "BURN_COMMAND='${z_command}'"    >> "${z_profile_file}"

  buc_step "BURN profile written: ${z_profile_file}"

  if test "${z_has_pubkey}" = "0"; then
    buh_e
    buh_section "SSH Key Required"
    buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_pubkey_file}"; local -r z_pubkey_yelp="${z_buym_yelp}"
    buh_line "No public key at ${z_pubkey_yelp}."
    buh_line   "Generate one:"
    buh_e
    buh_code   "ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f ~/.ssh/${z_alias}"
    buh_e
    buh_line   "Then re-run this constructor to populate the public key."
  fi
}

######################################################################
# Command Functions — Constructors

burn_construct_linux() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURN profile for Linux target"
  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Linux machine"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the remote host"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-linux)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zburn_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "linux" ""
}

burn_construct_mac() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURN profile for macOS target"
  buc_doc_param "host" "IP or hostname of the Mac"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on the remote host"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-mac)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zburn_construct "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "${3:-}" "mac" ""
}

burn_construct_localhost() {
  buc_doc_brief "Construct BURN profile for localhost (local SSH)"
  buc_doc_param "user" "Username on this machine"
  buc_doc_param "moniker" "Short name (alias = moniker-local)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  zburn_construct "localhost" "${1:-}" "${2:-}" "local" "" "id_ed25519"
}

######################################################################
# Command Functions — SSH Automation

burn_verify_ssh() {
  buc_doc_brief "Verify SSH connectivity to a BURN profile"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"

  buc_step "Testing SSH to ${BURN_ALIAS} (${BURN_HOST})..."
  local z_result=""
  if z_result=$(ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=10 "${BURN_ALIAS}" whoami 2>&1); then
    buc_success "SSH OK: ${BURN_ALIAS} -> ${z_result}"
  else
    buc_die "SSH FAILED: ${BURN_ALIAS} -> ${z_result}"
  fi
}

burn_install_key() {
  buc_doc_brief "Install BURN profile SSH key into authorized_keys (idempotent)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}" || buc_die "Profile alias required (e.g., winhost-cyg)"

  local -r z_auth_keys="${HOME}/.ssh/authorized_keys"

  zburn_build_key_line

  mkdir -p "${HOME}/.ssh"

  if test -f "${z_auth_keys}"; then
    local -r z_temp=$(mktemp)
    grep -v "${ZBURN_KEY_MARKER}" "${z_auth_keys}" > "${z_temp}" || true
    mv "${z_temp}" "${z_auth_keys}"
  fi

  printf '%s\n' "${ZBURN_KEY_LINE}" >> "${z_auth_keys}"
  chmod 600 "${z_auth_keys}"

  buc_success "Key installed for ${BURN_ALIAS} in ${z_auth_keys}"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zburn_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_CONFIG_DIR       " "BUK config directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burc_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burs_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burn_regime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bupr_PresentationRegime.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"

  zbuv_kindle
  zburd_kindle
  zburd_enforce

  source "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURC: ${BURD_REGIME_FILE}"

  zburc_kindle
  zburc_enforce

  source "${BURD_STATION_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURS: ${BURD_STATION_FILE}"

  zburs_kindle
  zburs_enforce

  zbupr_kindle

  # If BUZ_FOLIO is set, load and kindle the specified profile
  if test -n "${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"; then
    local -r z_profile_file="${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/users/${BURS_USER}/${BUZ_FOLIO}/burn.env"
    test -f "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "BURN profile not found: ${z_profile_file}"
    source "${z_profile_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source BURN: ${z_profile_file}"
    zburn_kindle
    zburn_enforce
  fi
}

buc_execute burn_ "Bash Utility Node Regime" zburn_furnish "$@"

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUW Zipper - Colophon registry for BUK workbench dispatch

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZBUWZ_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZBUWZ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Colophon registry initialization

zbuwz_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUWZ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "buwz already kindled"

  # Verify buz zipper is kindled (CLI furnish must kindle buz first)
  zbuz_sentinel

  # TabTarget subsystem (buut_cli.sh)
  local z_mod="buut_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_LIST_LAUNCHERS      "buw-tt-ll"  "${z_mod}" "buut_list_launchers"               ""  "List all registered launchers"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_BATCH_LOGGING       "buw-tt-cbl" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_batch_logging"      ""  "Create batch tabtarget with logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_BATCH_NOLOG         "buw-tt-cbn" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_batch_nolog"        ""  "Create batch tabtarget without logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_INTERACTIVE_LOGGING "buw-tt-cil" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_interactive_logging" ""  "Create interactive tabtarget with logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_INTERACTIVE_NOLOG   "buw-tt-cin" "${z_mod}" "buut_tabtarget_interactive_nolog"  ""  "Create interactive tabtarget without logging"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_TT_LAUNCHER            "buw-tt-cl"  "${z_mod}" "buut_launcher"                     ""  "Create launcher script"

  # Config Regime subsystem (burc_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burc_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_VALIDATE "buw-rcv" "${z_mod}" "burc_validate"  ""  "Validate BURC regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RC_RENDER   "buw-rcr" "${z_mod}" "burc_render"    ""  "Render BURC regime"

  # Station Regime subsystem (burs_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burs_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RS_VALIDATE "buw-rsv" "${z_mod}" "burs_validate"  ""  "Validate BURS regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RS_RENDER   "buw-rsr" "${z_mod}" "burs_render"    ""  "Render BURS regime"

  # Environment Regime subsystem (bure_cli.sh)
  z_mod="bure_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RE_VALIDATE "buw-rev" "${z_mod}" "bure_validate"  ""  "Validate BURE regime"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RE_RENDER   "buw-rer" "${z_mod}" "bure_render"    ""  "Render BURE regime"

  # Node Regime subsystem (burn_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burn_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_VALIDATE "buw-rnv" "${z_mod}" "burn_validate" "param1" "Validate BURN profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_RENDER   "buw-rnr" "${z_mod}" "burn_render"   "param1" "Render BURN profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_LIST     "buw-rnl" "${z_mod}" "burn_list"     ""       "List BURN profiles"

  # Qualification subsystem (buq_cli.sh)
  z_mod="buq_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_QUALIFY_SHELLCHECK "buw-qsc" "${z_mod}" "buq_shellcheck"  ""  "Run shellcheck on all tools"

  # Test fixtures (bux_cli.sh)
  z_mod="bux_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_DELAY "buw-xd" "${z_mod}" "bux_delay"  ""  "Sleep 20 seconds (timing fixture)"

  # Self-test (butt_testbench.sh)
  z_mod="butt_testbench.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_SELF_TEST "buw-st" "${z_mod}" "buw-st"  ""  "BUK self-test (kick-tires + bure-tweak)"

  # Node Regime — constructors + SSH automation (burn_cli.sh)
  z_mod="burn_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_LINUX      "buw-rhcl"  "${z_mod}" "burn_construct_linux"      ""        "Construct BURN profile: Linux"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_MAC        "buw-rhcm"  "${z_mod}" "burn_construct_mac"        ""        "Construct BURN profile: macOS"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RHC_LOCALHOST  "buw-rhcx"  "${z_mod}" "burn_construct_localhost"  ""        "Construct BURN profile: localhost"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_VERIFY_SSH  "buw-HWvs"  "${z_mod}" "burn_verify_ssh"           "param1"  "Verify SSH to BURN profile"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_RN_INSTALL_KEY "buw-rnk"   "${z_mod}" "burn_install_key"          "param1"  "Install BURN key into authorized_keys"

  # Handbook — top index + Windows OS procedures (buhw_cli.sh)
  z_mod="buhw_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_H0_TOP            "buw-h0"    "${z_mod}" "buhw_handbook_top"        ""        "BUK top-level handbook index"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_TOP            "buw-hw"    "${z_mod}" "buhw_top"                ""        "BUK-level Windows procedures checklist"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_BASE    "buw-HWab"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_access_base"        ""        "OpenSSH server install + lockdown"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_REMOTE  "buw-HWar"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_access_remote"      ""        "Client key generation"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ACCESS_ENTRY   "buw-HWax"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_access_entrypoints" ""        "SSH command= routing + icacls"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ENV_WSL        "buw-HWew"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_environment_wsl"    "param1"  "WSL distro creation"
  buz_enroll BUWZ_HW_ENV_CYGWIN     "buw-HWec"  "${z_mod}" "buhw_environment_cygwin" ""        "Cygwin install"

  readonly ZBUWZ_KINDLED=1
}

######################################################################
# Healthcheck (validates all enrolled tabtargets exist on disk)

zbuwz_healthcheck() {
  zbuwz_sentinel
  buz_healthcheck
}

######################################################################
# Internal sentinel

zbuwz_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUWZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buwz not kindled - call zbuwz_kindle first"
}

# eof

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_node_regime_list}
3	
4	NOTE: Lists every {burn_regime} profile in the local station subtree
5	without contacting any remote node.
6	Read-only enumeration scoped to the current {burs_user}.
7	
8	//axhoq_precondition
9	The station subtree at `.buk/users/{burs_user}/` exists and is readable
10	by the calling user.
11	
12	//axhos_step
13	. *Enumerate local profiles*: scan the station subtree for {burn_regime}
14	profile files
15	
16	//axhos_step
17	. *Display roster*: print each discovered {burn_alias} together with its
18	{burn_tier} classification
19	
20	//axhog_guarantee
21	No remote network contact occurred and no profile file was modified.
22	
23	//axhoc_completion
24	Exit 0 on successful enumeration; an empty roster is success.
25	Non-zero only when the station subtree cannot be read.
26

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_node_regime_render}
3	
4	NOTE: Prints the resolved field set for a single {burn_regime} profile
5	identified by a positional {burn_alias} argument.
6	Read-only; no remote contact.
7	
8	//axhopt_typed_parameter
9	{burn_alias}
10	
11	//axhoq_precondition
12	A {burn_regime} profile with the given {burn_alias} exists in the local
13	station subtree under the current {burs_user}.
14	
15	//axhos_step
16	. *Locate profile*: resolve the profile file for the supplied
17	{burn_alias} under the station subtree
18	
19	//axhos_step
20	. *Resolve fields*: parse the profile and emit every defined
21	{burn_regime} field assignment
22	
23	//axhog_guarantee
24	No remote network contact occurred and no field was modified on disk.
25	
26	//axhoc_completion
27	Exit 0 on successful render.
28	Non-zero when the {burn_alias} is not found or the profile fails to
29	parse.
30

1	//axhob_operation
2	{bust_node_regime_validate}
3	
4	NOTE: Validates a single {burn_regime} profile, identified by positional
5	{burn_alias}, for required-field presence, valid {burn_tier}, and
6	key-file accessibility.
7	Read-only; no remote contact.
8	
9	//axhopt_typed_parameter
10	{burn_alias}
11	
12	//axhoq_precondition
13	A {burn_regime} profile with the given {burn_alias} exists in the local
14	station subtree.
15	
16	//axhos_step
17	. *Load profile*: parse the profile file for the supplied {burn_alias}
18	
19	//axhos_step
20	. *Required fields*: confirm {burn_host}, {burn_user}, {burn_alias},
21	and {burn_tier} are present
22	
23	//axhos_step
24	. *Tier sanity*: confirm {burn_tier} is exactly one of
25	`privileged | workload`
26	
27	//axhos_step
28	. *Key reachable*: resolve {burn_key_file} and confirm the file is
29	readable by the calling user
30	
31	//axhog_guarantee
32	The verdict reflects the on-disk state at call time; no field was
33	modified.
34	
35	//axhoc_completion
36	Exit 0 when every check passes.
37	Non-zero with a diagnostic message identifying the first failed check.

260	
261	== Overview
262	
263	The Bash Utilities Specification defines the tabtarget dispatch system — the infrastructure for tab-completion-friendly launchers that route to kit formularies.
264	
265	BUK invented tabtargets; BUK defines them.
266	
267	For universal naming vocabulary (Cipher, Signet, Epithet, Inscription, Vesture), see VLS-VoxLiturgicalSpec.adoc.
268	
269	== Tabtarget Vesture
270	
271	[[bust_tabtarget]]
272	//axl_voices axo_entity
273	{bust_tabtarget}::
274	Vesture for tabtarget launcher identifiers.
275	Defines how launcher scripts in `tt/` are named.
276	+
277	* signet_case: lowercase with hyphen (the colophon)
278	* separator: `.`
279	* epithet_case: PascalCase (the frontispiece)
280	* envelope: `.sh`
281	+
282	Pattern: `{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
283	+
284	Examples:
285	+
286	* `rbw-cr.Rack.sh` — RBW workbench, Rack operation
287	* `rbw-cr.Rack.tadmor.sh` — same, with imprint specifying target
288	* `jjw-.HeatMount.sh` — JJK workbench, HeatMount operation
289	* `buw-tt.LauncherList.sh` — BUK tabtarget testbench, LauncherList operation
290	
291	== Dispatch
292	
293	The dispatch vocabulary defines the tabtarget launcher system.
294	A
295	{busdl_launcher}
296	validates arguments and delegates to a
297	{busdm_formulary}.
298	The
299	{busdc_colophon}
300	identifies the routing path;
301	the
302	{busdf_frontispiece}
303	provides human-readable description;
304	the
305	{busdi_imprint}
306	embeds target parameters.
307	
308	[[busdc_colophon]]
309	//axl_voices axt_string
310	{busdc_colophon}::
311	The routing identifier in a tabtarget inscription.
312	A
313	{busdc_colophon}
314	determines which
315	{busdm_formulary}
316	handles the dispatch.
317	Includes the hyphen as part of its identity (e.g., `rbw-`, `buw-tt-`).
318	+
319	The
320	{busdc_colophon}
321	is the first component of the tabtarget pattern, before the first dot separator.
322	+
323	Examples:
324	+
325	* `rbw-` routes to RBW workbench
326	* `jjw-` routes to JJK workbench
327	* `buw-tt-` routes to BUK tabtarget testbench
328	
329	[[busdf_frontispiece]]
330	//axl_voices axt_string
331	{busdf_frontispiece}::
332	The human-readable description in a tabtarget inscription.
333	A
334	{busdf_frontispiece}
335	uses PascalCase and describes the operation.
336	+
337	Appears after the
338	{busdc_colophon}
339	and dot separator.
340	+
341	Examples: `Build`, `Rack`, `RunVVX`, `HeatMount`.
342	
343	[[busdi_imprint]]
344	//axl_voices axt_string
345	{busdi_imprint}::
346	An optional embedded parameter in a tabtarget inscription.
347	An
348	{busdi_imprint}
349	specifies a target, instance, or configuration variant.
350	+
351	Appears after a second dot separator following the
352	{busdf_frontispiece}.
353	+
354	Examples:
355	+
356	* `tadmor` in `rbw-cr.Rack.tadmor.sh`
357	* `srjcl` in `rbw-cr.Rack.srjcl.sh`
358	
359	[[busdm_formulary]]
360	//axl_voices axo_entity
361	{busdm_formulary}::
362	A component that routes
363	{busdc_colophon_s}
364	to implementations.
365	Each
366	{busdm_formulary}
367	registers handlers for a set of
368	{busdc_colophon_s}
369	and dispatches to the appropriate function.
370	+
371	Types:
372	+
373	* **Workbench** (`{prefix}w_workbench.sh`): Routes commands for a kit
374	* **Testbench** (`{prefix}t_testbench.sh`): Routes tests for a kit
375	+
376	A
377	{busdm_formulary}
378	is sourced by the
379	{busdl_launcher}
380	and provides the dispatch function.
381	
382	[[busdl_launcher]]
383	//axl_voices axo_entity
384	{busdl_launcher}::
385	A bootstrap script in the `tt/` directory that validates arguments and delegates to a
386	{busdm_formulary}.
387	A
388	{busdl_launcher}
389	is the entry point for tabtarget invocation.
390	+
391	Naming follows
392	{bust_tabtarget}:
393	`{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
394	+
395	Responsibilities:
396	+
397	* Parse
398	{busdc_colophon}
399	from own filename
400	* Validate required arguments
401	* Source the target
402	{busdm_formulary}
403	* Invoke the dispatched function
404	
405	== Routing Behavior
406	
407	=== Dispatch Flow
408	
409	. User invokes launcher: `./tt/rbw-cr.Rack.tadmor.sh`
410	. Launcher parses its filename to extract:
411	* {busdc_colophon}:
412	`rbw-cr`
413	* {busdf_frontispiece}:
414	`Rack`
415	* {busdi_imprint}:
416	`tadmor`
417	. Launcher sources the appropriate
418	{busdm_formulary}
419	(`rbw_workbench.sh`)
420	. Formulary's dispatch function routes based on frontispiece
421	. Target function executes with imprint as parameter
422	
423	=== Workbench Pattern
424	
425	[source,bash]
426	----
427	# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}w_workbench.sh
428	
429	{prefix}w_dispatch() {
430	    local frontispiece="$1"
431	    local imprint="$2"
432	    shift 2
433	
434	    case "$frontispiece" in
435	        Build)          {prefix}w_build "$imprint" "$@" ;;
436	        Rack)           {prefix}w_rack "$imprint" "$@" ;;
437	        *)              buc_fatal "Unknown frontispiece: $frontispiece" ;;
438	    esac
439	}
440	----
441	
442	=== Testbench Pattern
443	
444	Testbenches follow the same pattern but route to test functions:
445	
446	[source,bash]
447	----
448	# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}t_testbench.sh
449	
450	{prefix}t_dispatch() {
451	    local frontispiece="$1"
452	    shift
453	
454	    case "$frontispiece" in
455	        Unit)        {prefix}t_unit "$@" ;;
456	        Integration) {prefix}t_integration "$@" ;;
457	        *)           buc_fatal "Unknown test: $frontispiece" ;;
458	    esac
459	}
460	----
461	
462	=== Test Registry Model
463	
464	The test registry (`butr_registry.sh`) uses two orthogonal enrollment dimensions:
465	
466	**Fixture** — init/setup execution context.
467	Each test case belongs to exactly one fixture.
468	Fixtures provide isolation via `_tsuite` subshell boundaries.
469	Registered with `butr_fixture_enroll(name, init_fn, setup_fn)`.
470	
471	**Suite** — cross-cutting selection group (N:M with cases).
472	A case can belong to multiple suites.
473	Suites classify by dependency tier (what environment must exist), not by test methodology.
474	A suite is a membership tag.
475	Suites enable suite dispatch: run a cross-cutting subset of cases grouped by their owning fixtures.
476	Suite context is set with `butr_suite_enroll(SUITE1 [SUITE2...])` before case enrollments.
477	Queried by `butd_run_suite(suite_name)`.
478	
479	Standard suite constants (defined as `readonly` in testbench kindle):
480	
481	* `BUTR_SUITE_FAST` — no external dependencies, CI-safe
482	* `BUTR_SUITE_SERVICE` — needs credentials, no container runtime
483	* `BUTR_SUITE_COMPLETE` — full test matrix including container runtime
484	
485	Dispatch entry points:
486	
487	* `butd_run_all()` — all fixtures, all cases
488	* `butd_run_fixture(name [case...])` — single fixture, optional case filter
489	* `butd_run_one(fn)` — single case with its fixture setup
490	* `butd_run_suite(suite)` — cross-cutting suite, grouped by fixture
491	
492	=== Colophon Conventions
493	
494	{busdc_colophon_s}
495	follow a structure that encodes both kit identity and formulary type:
496	
497	* `{cipher}-` — default workbench (e.g., `rbw-`, `jjw-`)
498	* `{cipher}-t-` — testbench (e.g., `buw-t-`, `jjw-t-`)
499	* `{cipher}-{variant}-` — specialized workbench (e.g., `rbw-cr-` for crucible rack operations)
500	
501	The trailing hyphen is always part of the colophon — it separates the routing prefix from the dot-delimited tabtarget components.
502	
503	== Test Invocation
504	
505	The test invocation vocabulary defines how test cases exercise code under controlled capture.
506	All invocation tiers share a common primitive,
507	`zbuto_invoke`,
508	which captures stdout, stderr, exit status, and optional BURV output into
509	{busig_stdout},
510	{busig_stderr},
511	{busig_status},
512	and
513	{busig_burv_output}.
514	
515	=== Invocation Tiers
516	
517	Three tiers provide increasing levels of resolution:
518	
519	[[busit_unit]]
520	//axl_voices axo_entity
521	{busit_unit}::
522	Invokes a raw function or command directly via `zbuto_invoke`.
523	No tabtarget file or workbench routing involved.
524	+
525	API: `buto_unit_expect_ok(command...)`, `buto_unit_expect_fatal(command...)`, `buto_unit_expect_ok_stdout(expected, command...)`
526	
527	[[busit_tabtarget]]
528	//axl_voices axo_entity
529	{busit_tabtarget}::
530	Resolves a
531	{busdc_colophon}
532	to a
533	{busdl_launcher}
534	file in `tt/`, then invokes it via `zbuto_invoke`.
535	Dies if no tabtarget matches or if multiple matches exist.
536	+
537	API: `buto_tt_expect_ok(colophon, args...)`, `buto_tt_expect_fatal(colophon, args...)`
538	
539	[[busit_launch]]
540	//axl_voices axo_entity
541	{busit_launch}::
542	Invokes a workbench dispatch function directly, bypassing the
543	{busdl_launcher}
544	file.
545	Useful when exercising workbench routing without a tabtarget on disk.
546	+
547	API: `buto_launch_expect_ok(launcher, colophon, args...)`, `buto_launch_expect_fatal(launcher, colophon, args...)`
548	
549	=== Capture Globals
550	
551	Every call to `zbuto_invoke` sets four globals that subsequent assertions can inspect.
552	
553	[[busig_stdout]]
554	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
555	{busig_stdout}::
556	Captured stdout from the invoked command.
557	
558	[[busig_stderr]]
559	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
560	{busig_stderr}::
561	Captured stderr from the invoked command.
562	
563	[[busig_status]]
564	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
565	{busig_status}::
566	Exit code from the invoked command.
567	
568	[[busig_burv_output]]
569	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
570	{busig_burv_output}::
571	BURV output directory for the invocation.
572	Empty when
573	{busx_burv_root}
574	is not set.
575	When set, contains the invocation's isolated output tree rooted at
576	`{BUTE_BURV_ROOT}/invoke-NNNNN/output`.
577	
578	=== Case Boundary
579	
580	[[busx_tcase]]
581	//axl_voices axo_entity
582	{busx_tcase}::
583	The case isolation runner (`zbute_tcase`).
584	Executes a single test case function in a subshell with per-case temporary state.
585	Sets
586	{busx_temp_dir}
587	and
588	{busx_burv_root}
589	before invoking the case function.
590	+
591	The subshell boundary ensures that variable mutations, function redefinitions, and side effects in one case cannot leak to another.
592	
593	[[busx_temp_dir]]
594	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
595	{busx_temp_dir}::
596	Per-case temporary directory (`BUT_TEMP_DIR`), set by
597	{busx_tcase}.
598	Created as a subdirectory of the fixture's root temp dir, named after the case function.
599	
600	[[busx_burv_root]]

= Bash Utilities Specification
:doctype: book

// tag::mapping-section[]
//
// Category declarations:
// bust_:  Tabtarget (launcher naming vesture)
// busd:   Dispatch (non-terminal, tabtarget system umbrella)
// busdc_: Dispatch Colophon (routing identifier)
// busdf_: Dispatch Frontispiece (human-readable description)
// busdi_: Dispatch Imprint (embedded parameter/target specifier)
// busdm_: Dispatch Formulary (routing component)
// busdl_: Dispatch Launcher (bootstrap script)
// bukro_: Kit Regime Operation (kindle/validate/render)
// burc_:  Configuration Regime (project-level settings)
// burn_:  Node Regime (per-node SSH connection profiles, multi-instance)
// burs_:  Station Regime (developer-level settings)
// burd_:  Dispatch Runtime (ephemeral state assembled during dispatch)
// bure_:  Environment Regime (ambient caller-supplied behavioral overrides)
// busi:   Invocation (test invocation umbrella)
// busn_:  Node Access (verbs for remote node management)
// busit_: Invocation Tier (tier classification)
// busig_: Invocation Global (capture state set by zbuto_invoke)
// busx_:  Case Boundary (test case isolation runner)
// busff_: Fact-File (inter-step communication via output files)
// buf_:   Fact Module (dual-write fact-file primitives)
// busy_:  Yelp (pre-rendered formatted string capture)
// burx_:  Exchange Regime (cross-project dispatch state)
//
// Variant patterns:
//   _s: plural form
//   _p: possessive form
//

== Overview

The Bash Utilities Specification defines the tabtarget dispatch system — the infrastructure for tab-completion-friendly launchers that route to kit formularies.

BUK invented tabtargets; BUK defines them.

For universal naming vocabulary (Cipher, Signet, Epithet, Inscription, Vesture), see VLS-VoxLiturgicalSpec.adoc.

== Tabtarget Vesture

[[bust_tabtarget]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{bust_tabtarget}::
Vesture for tabtarget launcher identifiers.
Defines how launcher scripts in `tt/` are named.
+
* signet_case: lowercase with hyphen (the colophon)
* separator: `.`
* epithet_case: PascalCase (the frontispiece)
* envelope: `.sh`
+
Pattern: `{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
+
Examples:
+
* `rbw-cr.Rack.sh` — RBW workbench, Rack operation
* `rbw-cr.Rack.tadmor.sh` — same, with imprint specifying target
* `jjw-.HeatMount.sh` — JJK workbench, HeatMount operation
* `buw-tt.LauncherList.sh` — BUK tabtarget testbench, LauncherList operation

== Dispatch

The dispatch vocabulary defines the tabtarget launcher system.
A
{busdl_launcher}
validates arguments and delegates to a
{busdm_formulary}.
The
{busdc_colophon}
identifies the routing path;
the
{busdf_frontispiece}
provides human-readable description;
the
{busdi_imprint}
embeds target parameters.

[[busdc_colophon]]
//axl_voices axt_string
{busdc_colophon}::
The routing identifier in a tabtarget inscription.
A
{busdc_colophon}
determines which
{busdm_formulary}
handles the dispatch.
Includes the hyphen as part of its identity (e.g., `rbw-`, `buw-tt-`).
+
The
{busdc_colophon}
is the first component of the tabtarget pattern, before the first dot separator.
+
Examples:
+
* `rbw-` routes to RBW workbench
* `jjw-` routes to JJK workbench
* `buw-tt-` routes to BUK tabtarget testbench

[[busdf_frontispiece]]
//axl_voices axt_string
{busdf_frontispiece}::
The human-readable description in a tabtarget inscription.
A
{busdf_frontispiece}
uses PascalCase and describes the operation.
+
Appears after the
{busdc_colophon}
and dot separator.
+
Examples: `Build`, `Rack`, `RunVVX`, `HeatMount`.

[[busdi_imprint]]
//axl_voices axt_string
{busdi_imprint}::
An optional embedded parameter in a tabtarget inscription.
An
{busdi_imprint}
specifies a target, instance, or configuration variant.
+
Appears after a second dot separator following the
{busdf_frontispiece}.
+
Examples:
+
* `tadmor` in `rbw-cr.Rack.tadmor.sh`
* `srjcl` in `rbw-cr.Rack.srjcl.sh`

[[busdm_formulary]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busdm_formulary}::
A component that routes
{busdc_colophon_s}
to implementations.
Each
{busdm_formulary}
registers handlers for a set of
{busdc_colophon_s}
and dispatches to the appropriate function.
+
Types:
+
* **Workbench** (`{prefix}w_workbench.sh`): Routes commands for a kit
* **Testbench** (`{prefix}t_testbench.sh`): Routes tests for a kit
+
A
{busdm_formulary}
is sourced by the
{busdl_launcher}
and provides the dispatch function.

[[busdl_launcher]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busdl_launcher}::
A bootstrap script in the `tt/` directory that validates arguments and delegates to a
{busdm_formulary}.
A
{busdl_launcher}
is the entry point for tabtarget invocation.
+
Naming follows
{bust_tabtarget}:
`{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
+
Responsibilities:
+
* Parse
{busdc_colophon}
from own filename
* Validate required arguments
* Source the target
{busdm_formulary}
* Invoke the dispatched function

== Routing Behavior

=== Dispatch Flow

. User invokes launcher: `./tt/rbw-cr.Rack.tadmor.sh`
. Launcher parses its filename to extract:
* {busdc_colophon}:
`rbw-cr`
* {busdf_frontispiece}:
`Rack`
* {busdi_imprint}:
`tadmor`
. Launcher sources the appropriate
{busdm_formulary}
(`rbw_workbench.sh`)
. Formulary's dispatch function routes based on frontispiece
. Target function executes with imprint as parameter

=== Workbench Pattern

[source,bash]
----
# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}w_workbench.sh

{prefix}w_dispatch() {
    local frontispiece="$1"
    local imprint="$2"
    shift 2

    case "$frontispiece" in
        Build)          {prefix}w_build "$imprint" "$@" ;;
        Rack)           {prefix}w_rack "$imprint" "$@" ;;
        *)              buc_fatal "Unknown frontispiece: $frontispiece" ;;
    esac
}
----

=== Testbench Pattern

Testbenches follow the same pattern but route to test functions:

[source,bash]
----
# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}t_testbench.sh

{prefix}t_dispatch() {
    local frontispiece="$1"
    shift

    case "$frontispiece" in
        Unit)        {prefix}t_unit "$@" ;;
        Integration) {prefix}t_integration "$@" ;;
        *)           buc_fatal "Unknown test: $frontispiece" ;;
    esac
}
----

=== Test Registry Model

The test registry (`butr_registry.sh`) uses two orthogonal enrollment dimensions:

**Fixture** — init/setup execution context.
Each test case belongs to exactly one fixture.
Fixtures provide isolation via `_tsuite` subshell boundaries.
Registered with `butr_fixture_enroll(name, init_fn, setup_fn)`.

**Suite** — cross-cutting selection group (N:M with cases).
A case can belong to multiple suites.
Suites classify by dependency tier (what environment must exist), not by test methodology.
A suite is a membership tag.
Suites enable suite dispatch: run a cross-cutting subset of cases grouped by their owning fixtures.
Suite context is set with `butr_suite_enroll(SUITE1 [SUITE2...])` before case enrollments.
Queried by `butd_run_suite(suite_name)`.

Standard suite constants (defined as `readonly` in testbench kindle):

* `BUTR_SUITE_FAST` — no external dependencies, CI-safe
* `BUTR_SUITE_SERVICE` — needs credentials, no container runtime
* `BUTR_SUITE_COMPLETE` — full test matrix including container runtime

Dispatch entry points:

* `butd_run_all()` — all fixtures, all cases
* `butd_run_fixture(name [case...])` — single fixture, optional case filter
* `butd_run_one(fn)` — single case with its fixture setup
* `butd_run_suite(suite)` — cross-cutting suite, grouped by fixture

=== Colophon Conventions

{busdc_colophon_s}
follow a structure that encodes both kit identity and formulary type:

* `{cipher}-` — default workbench (e.g., `rbw-`, `jjw-`)
* `{cipher}-t-` — testbench (e.g., `buw-t-`, `jjw-t-`)
* `{cipher}-{variant}-` — specialized workbench (e.g., `rbw-cr-` for crucible rack operations)

The trailing hyphen is always part of the colophon — it separates the routing prefix from the dot-delimited tabtarget components.

== Test Invocation

The test invocation vocabulary defines how test cases exercise code under controlled capture.
All invocation tiers share a common primitive,
`zbuto_invoke`,
which captures stdout, stderr, exit status, and optional BURV output into
{busig_stdout},
{busig_stderr},
{busig_status},
and
{busig_burv_output}.

=== Invocation Tiers

Three tiers provide increasing levels of resolution:

[[busit_unit]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busit_unit}::
Invokes a raw function or command directly via `zbuto_invoke`.
No tabtarget file or workbench routing involved.
+
API: `buto_unit_expect_ok(command...)`, `buto_unit_expect_fatal(command...)`, `buto_unit_expect_ok_stdout(expected, command...)`

[[busit_tabtarget]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busit_tabtarget}::
Resolves a
{busdc_colophon}
to a
{busdl_launcher}
file in `tt/`, then invokes it via `zbuto_invoke`.
Dies if no tabtarget matches or if multiple matches exist.
+
API: `buto_tt_expect_ok(colophon, args...)`, `buto_tt_expect_fatal(colophon, args...)`

[[busit_launch]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busit_launch}::
Invokes a workbench dispatch function directly, bypassing the
{busdl_launcher}
file.
Useful when exercising workbench routing without a tabtarget on disk.
+
API: `buto_launch_expect_ok(launcher, colophon, args...)`, `buto_launch_expect_fatal(launcher, colophon, args...)`

=== Capture Globals

Every call to `zbuto_invoke` sets four globals that subsequent assertions can inspect.

[[busig_stdout]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{busig_stdout}::
Captured stdout from the invoked command.

[[busig_stderr]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{busig_stderr}::
Captured stderr from the invoked command.

[[busig_status]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{busig_status}::
Exit code from the invoked command.

[[busig_burv_output]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{busig_burv_output}::
BURV output directory for the invocation.
Empty when
{busx_burv_root}
is not set.
When set, contains the invocation's isolated output tree rooted at
`{BUTE_BURV_ROOT}/invoke-NNNNN/output`.

=== Case Boundary

[[busx_tcase]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busx_tcase}::
The case isolation runner (`zbute_tcase`).
Executes a single test case function in a subshell with per-case temporary state.
Sets
{busx_temp_dir}
and
{busx_burv_root}
before invoking the case function.
+
The subshell boundary ensures that variable mutations, function redefinitions, and side effects in one case cannot leak to another.

[[busx_temp_dir]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{busx_temp_dir}::
Per-case temporary directory (`BUT_TEMP_DIR`), set by
{busx_tcase}.
Created as a subdirectory of the fixture's root temp dir, named after the case function.

[[busx_burv_root]]
