820	
821	[[burs_prefix]]
822	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
823	{burs_prefix}::
824	Bash Utility Station Regime
825	
826	[[burn_prefix]]
827	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
828	{burn_prefix}::
829	Bash Utility Regime Node
830	
831	[[bure_prefix]]
832	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
833	{bure_prefix}::
834	Bash Utility Environment Regime
835	
836	=== Kit Regime Operations
837	
838	[[bukro_kindle]]
839	//axvr_kindle
840	{bukro_kindle}::
841	Silent initialization of Bash Utility Kit regime state from
842	{axrg_assignment_s}.
843	Sets defaults, detects unexpected variables, builds derived structures.
844	
845	[[bukro_validate]]
846	//axvr_validate
847	{bukro_validate}::
848	Strict field validation for Bash Utility Kit
849	{axrg_regime_s}.
850	Checks all type and constraint requirements via buv_env_* validators.
851	
852	[[bukro_render]]
853	//axvr_render
854	{bukro_render}::
855	Diagnostic display of Bash Utility Kit
856	{axrg_regime}
857	state.
858	
859	=== BURC Regime
860	
861	[[burc_regime]]
862	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton
863	{burc_regime}::
864	Configuration regime for project-level BUK settings.
865	BURC assignment files in `.buk/burc.env` define repository structure and tabtarget infrastructure.
866	
867	[[burc_station_file]]
868	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
869	{burc_station_file}::
870	Path to the developer's personal
871	{burs_regime}
872	assignment file.
873	
874	[[burc_tabtarget_dir]]
875	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
876	{burc_tabtarget_dir}::
877	Directory containing
878	{busdl_launcher}
879	scripts.
880	
881	[[burc_tabtarget_delimiter]]
882	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
883	{burc_tabtarget_delimiter}::
884	Single-character token separator in
885	{bust_tabtarget}
886	inscriptions.
887	
888	[[burc_tools_dir]]
889	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
890	{burc_tools_dir}::
891	Directory containing BUK utilities and project tool scripts.
892	
893	[[burc_project_root]]
894	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
895	{burc_project_root}::
896	Path from assignment file location to the project root directory.
897	
898	[[burc_managed_kits]]
899	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
900	{burc_managed_kits}::
901	Comma-separated list of kits managed by vvx.
902	At release time, determines which kits are built into the binary.
903	At install time, must exactly match the parcel's kit list.
904	
905	[[burc_temp_root_dir]]
906	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
907	{burc_temp_root_dir}::
908	Root directory for BUK temporary files.
909	
910	[[burc_output_root_dir]]
911	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
912	{burc_output_root_dir}::
913	Root directory for BUK command output files.
914	
915	[[burc_log_last]]
916	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
917	{burc_log_last}::
918	Filename stem for the most recent command execution log.
919	
920	[[burc_log_ext]]
921	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
922	{burc_log_ext}::
923	File extension for log files, without leading dot.
924	
925	=== BURS Regime
926	
927	[[burs_regime]]
928	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton
929	{burs_regime}::
930	Configuration regime for developer-level BUK settings.
931	BURS assignment files are personal to each developer and not committed to version control.
932	
933	[[burs_user]]
934	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
935	{burs_user}::
936	Local developer username.
937	Routes to the correct `.buk/users/` subdirectory for
938	{burn_regime}
939	profiles.
940	
941	[[burs_log_dir]]
942	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
943	{burs_log_dir}::
944	Directory where this developer stores BUK operation logs.
945	
946	=== BURN Regime
947	
948	[[burn_regime]]
949	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
950	{burn_regime}::
951	Per-node configuration regime describing the shape of a remote target.
952	A
953	{burn_regime}
954	instance is identified by its
955	{burn_viceroyalty}
956	(directory name) and is shared across station users (git-transported);
957	it carries no key material and changes rarely.
958	+
959	{burn_regime}
960	is the sole home of node address-of-record per
961	{burn_host_singularity};
962	{burp_regime}
963	and
964	{burw_regime}
965	instances reference it via
966	{burp_node}
967	and
968	{burw_node}.
969	
970	[[burn_host]]
971	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
972	{burn_host}::
973	IP address or hostname of the remote node.
974	
975	[[burn_os_family]]
976	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
977	{burn_os_family}::
978	Operating-system family of the node: `linux`, `mac`, or `windows`.
979	Drives platform-specific procedure dispatch (OS-user creation
980	mechanism, file-permission tooling, service-restart command).
981	
982	[[burn_workloads]]
983	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string axd_repeated
984	{burn_workloads}::
985	Authoritative template of valid
986	{burw_lieutenancy}
987	strings on this node.
988	{busn_conscript}
989	may only materialize a workload whose alias is enumerated here;
990	the operator pre-declares the node's supported workload offices.
991	
992	[[burn_user]]
993	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
994	{burn_user}::
995	Username on the remote node.
996	May differ from the local
997	{burs_user}.
998	
999	[[burn_alias]]
1000	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
1001	{burn_alias}::
1002	SSH alias and key filename.
1003	Must match the directory name containing this profile's assignment file.
1004	
1005	[[burn_ssh_pubkey]]
1006	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
1007	{burn_ssh_pubkey}::
1008	Full SSH public key line (e.g., `ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host`).
1009	Git-transported: no private key material.
1010	Used by handbook procedures to render `authorized_keys` entries and `ssh-keygen` commands.
1011	
1012	[[burn_key_file]]
1013	//axvr_variable axd_optional axtu_string
1014	{burn_key_file}::
1015	SSH private key filename within `~/.ssh/`.
1016	Empty defaults to {burn_alias} (one key per connection).
1017	Localhost profiles set `id_ed25519` to share the invoking user's default key.
1018	
1019	[[burn_command]]
1020	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
1021	{burn_command}::
1022	Shell command for `command=` routing in SSH `authorized_keys`.
1023	Empty for direct-shell connections (Linux, macOS, localhost — no routing needed).
1024	When set, determines which shell or environment the connection launches
1025	(e.g., `C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe -l` for Cygwin, `wsl.exe -d Ubuntu` for WSL).
1026	
1027	[[burn_tier]]
1028	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
1029	{burn_tier}::
1030	Privilege classification of this profile.
1031	Values: `privileged` (admin-level operations on the node — sshd configuration,
1032	user provisioning, node-level setup) or `workload` (normal remote work as an
1033	ephemeral user on an established node).
1034	Tabtargets enforce tier preconditions at entry: privileged-only operations
1035	refuse workload aliases and vice versa.
1036	
1037	=== BURN/BURP/BURW Identifier Vocabulary
1038	
1039	The following quoins establish the minted-name vocabulary for the
1040	BURN/BURP/BURW model.
1041	
1042	[[burn_viceroyalty]]
1043	//axl_voices axo_entity
1044	{burn_viceroyalty}::
1045	The minted identifier of a
1046	{burn_regime}
1047	instance.
1048	{burp_regime}
1049	and
1050	{burw_regime}
1051	instances reference a node by its
1052	{burn_viceroyalty}.
1053	+
1054	The
1055	{burn_viceroyalty}
1056	is conveyed by the directory containing the
1057	{burn_regime}
1058	assignment file;
1059	it is not redundantly inscribed as a field within the file.
1060	+
1061	Examples: `redbox`, `winbox-prod`, `hawthorne`.
1062	
1063	[[burp_investiture]]
1064	//axl_voices axo_entity
1065	{burp_investiture}::
1066	The minted identifier of a
1067	{burp_regime}
1068	instance — the operator's named grant of privileged authority over a
1069	{burn_viceroyalty}.
1070	+
1071	The
1072	{burp_investiture}
1073	is conveyed by the directory containing the
1074	{burp_regime}
1075	assignment file;
1076	it is not redundantly inscribed as a field within the file.
1077	+
1078	Examples: `redbox-bhyslop-admin`, `winbox-prod-alice-admin`.
1079	
1080	[[burw_lieutenancy]]
1081	//axl_voices axo_entity
1082	{burw_lieutenancy}::
1083	The minted identifier of a
1084	{burw_regime}
1085	instance — a workload office operating on a
1086	{burn_viceroyalty}
1087	under the authority of a
1088	{burp_investiture}.
1089	A
1090	{burw_lieutenancy}
1091	names a workload entry (shell command, remote OS user, key file) that
1092	materializes when a workload is conscripted.
1093	+
1094	The
1095	{burw_lieutenancy}
1096	is conveyed by the directory containing the
1097	{burw_regime}
1098	state file;
1099	it is not redundantly inscribed as a field within the file.
1100	+
1101	The
1102	{burw_lieutenancy}
1103	string is by construction the remote OS username of its workload
1104	account on the node, drawn at conscript time from the
1105	{burn_viceroyalty}'s authoritative `burn_workloads` template.
1106	+
1107	Examples: `redbox-cyg`, `redbox-wsl`, `redbox-pwsh`.
1108	
1109	[[burn_host_singularity]]
1110	//axl_voices axk_premise
1111	{burn_host_singularity}::
1112	The host of a node lives only in
1113	{burn_regime}.
1114	{burp_regime}
1115	and
1116	{burw_regime}
1117	instances reach the host by reference
1118	(via
1119	{burp_node}
1120	and
1121	{burw_node}
1122	respectively) and never duplicate it.
1123	A given node has exactly one address-of-record;
1124	credentials and workload state never re-state it.
1125	
1126	[[bus_keys_operator_owned]]
1127	//axl_voices axk_premise
1128	{bus_keys_operator_owned}::
1129	The system never generates or modifies SSH key material.
1130	The operator owns all key administration: keypair creation,
1131	distribution of pubkeys into regime files, and placement of private
1132	keys on the operator's station.
1133	Regime files carry only the public-key line;
1134	private keys live at operator-managed paths and are never written by
1135	BUK procedures.
1136	
1137	=== BURP Regime
1138	
1139	[[burp_prefix]]
1140	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
1141	{burp_prefix}::
1142	Bash Utility Regime Privileged
1143	
1144	[[burp_regime]]
1145	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
1146	{burp_regime}::
1147	Per-station-user privileged-credential regime.
1148	A
1149	{burp_regime}
1150	instance is identified by its
1151	{burp_investiture}
1152	(directory name).
1153	+
1154	{burp_regime}
1155	references its node by
1156	{burp_node}
1157	per
1158	{burn_host_singularity}.
1159	
1160	[[burp_node]]
1161	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
1162	{burp_node}::
1163	Reference to a
1164	{burn_viceroyalty}
1165	— the node this investiture is granted over.
1166	Resolves to the
1167	{burn_regime}
1168	file at the directory bearing this name.
1169	
1170	[[burp_user]]
1171	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
1172	{burp_user}::
1173	Remote admin OS user this credential authenticates as
1174	(e.g., `Administrator` on Windows, `root` or a sudo-capable user on
1175	Linux/Mac).
1176	
1177	[[burp_ssh_pubkey]]
1178	//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
1179	{burp_ssh_pubkey}::
1180	Full SSH public key line of the operator-managed admin keypair.
1181	The matching private key lives at `${BURS_KEY_DIR}/<{burp_investiture}>` on
1182	the operator's station per
1183	{bus_keys_operator_owned}.
1184	
1185	[[burp_command]]
1186	//axvr_variable axd_optional axtu_string
1187	{burp_command}::
1188	Shell command for SSH `command=` routing in the node's
1189	`administrators_authorized_keys`.
1190	Empty for direct-shell connections;
1191	non-empty when the admin shell is selected at the SSH layer
1192	(e.g., `powershell.exe`, `C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe -l`).
1193	
1194	=== BURW Regime
1195	
1196	[[burw_prefix]]
1197	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
1198	{burw_prefix}::
1199	Bash Utility Regime Workload
1200	
1201	[[burw_regime]]
1202	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
1203	{burw_regime}::
1204	Per-station-user workload-state regime — a record of a workload office
1205	materialized on a node by
1206	{busn_conscript}.
1207	A
1208	{burw_regime}
1209	instance is identified by its
1210	{burw_lieutenancy}
1211	(directory name).
1212	Unlike
1213	{burp_regime}
1214	(operator-authored config),
1215	{burw_regime}
1216	is system-written by
1217	{busn_conscript}
1218	and consumed by
1219	{busn_discharge},

[[burs_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burs_prefix}::
Bash Utility Station Regime

[[burn_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burn_prefix}::
Bash Utility Regime Node

[[bure_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{bure_prefix}::
Bash Utility Environment Regime

=== Kit Regime Operations

[[bukro_kindle]]
//axvr_kindle
{bukro_kindle}::
Silent initialization of Bash Utility Kit regime state from
{axrg_assignment_s}.
Sets defaults, detects unexpected variables, builds derived structures.

[[bukro_validate]]
//axvr_validate
{bukro_validate}::
Strict field validation for Bash Utility Kit
{axrg_regime_s}.
Checks all type and constraint requirements via buv_env_* validators.

[[bukro_render]]
//axvr_render
{bukro_render}::
Diagnostic display of Bash Utility Kit
{axrg_regime}
state.

=== BURC Regime

[[burc_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton
{burc_regime}::
Configuration regime for project-level BUK settings.
BURC assignment files in `.buk/burc.env` define repository structure and tabtarget infrastructure.

[[burc_station_file]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burc_station_file}::
Path to the developer's personal
{burs_regime}
assignment file.

[[burc_tabtarget_dir]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burc_tabtarget_dir}::
Directory containing
{busdl_launcher}
scripts.

[[burc_tabtarget_delimiter]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burc_tabtarget_delimiter}::
Single-character token separator in
{bust_tabtarget}
inscriptions.

[[burc_tools_dir]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burc_tools_dir}::
Directory containing BUK utilities and project tool scripts.

[[burc_project_root]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burc_project_root}::
Path from assignment file location to the project root directory.

[[burc_managed_kits]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burc_managed_kits}::
Comma-separated list of kits managed by vvx.
At release time, determines which kits are built into the binary.
At install time, must exactly match the parcel's kit list.

[[burc_temp_root_dir]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burc_temp_root_dir}::
Root directory for BUK temporary files.

[[burc_output_root_dir]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burc_output_root_dir}::
Root directory for BUK command output files.

[[burc_log_last]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burc_log_last}::
Filename stem for the most recent command execution log.

[[burc_log_ext]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burc_log_ext}::
File extension for log files, without leading dot.

=== BURS Regime

[[burs_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced axrd_singleton
{burs_regime}::
Configuration regime for developer-level BUK settings.
BURS assignment files are personal to each developer and not committed to version control.

[[burs_user]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burs_user}::
Local developer username.
Routes to the correct `.buk/users/` subdirectory for
{burn_regime}
profiles.

[[burs_log_dir]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_path
{burs_log_dir}::
Directory where this developer stores BUK operation logs.

=== BURN Regime

[[burn_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
{burn_regime}::
Per-node configuration regime describing the shape of a remote target.
A
{burn_regime}
instance is identified by its
{burn_viceroyalty}
(directory name) and is shared across station users (git-transported);
it carries no key material and changes rarely.
+
{burn_regime}
is the sole home of node address-of-record per
{burn_host_singularity};
{burp_regime}
and
{burw_regime}
instances reference it via
{burp_node}
and
{burw_node}.

[[burn_host]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burn_host}::
IP address or hostname of the remote node.

[[burn_os_family]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burn_os_family}::
Operating-system family of the node: `linux`, `mac`, or `windows`.
Drives platform-specific procedure dispatch (OS-user creation
mechanism, file-permission tooling, service-restart command).

[[burn_workloads]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string axd_repeated
{burn_workloads}::
Authoritative template of valid
{burw_lieutenancy}
strings on this node.
{busn_conscript}
may only materialize a workload whose alias is enumerated here;
the operator pre-declares the node's supported workload offices.

[[burn_user]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burn_user}::
Username on the remote node.
May differ from the local
{burs_user}.

[[burn_alias]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burn_alias}::
SSH alias and key filename.
Must match the directory name containing this profile's assignment file.

[[burn_ssh_pubkey]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burn_ssh_pubkey}::
Full SSH public key line (e.g., `ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host`).
Git-transported: no private key material.
Used by handbook procedures to render `authorized_keys` entries and `ssh-keygen` commands.

[[burn_key_file]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional axtu_string
{burn_key_file}::
SSH private key filename within `~/.ssh/`.
Empty defaults to {burn_alias} (one key per connection).
Localhost profiles set `id_ed25519` to share the invoking user's default key.

[[burn_command]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burn_command}::
Shell command for `command=` routing in SSH `authorized_keys`.
Empty for direct-shell connections (Linux, macOS, localhost — no routing needed).
When set, determines which shell or environment the connection launches
(e.g., `C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe -l` for Cygwin, `wsl.exe -d Ubuntu` for WSL).

[[burn_tier]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burn_tier}::
Privilege classification of this profile.
Values: `privileged` (admin-level operations on the node — sshd configuration,
user provisioning, node-level setup) or `workload` (normal remote work as an
ephemeral user on an established node).
Tabtargets enforce tier preconditions at entry: privileged-only operations
refuse workload aliases and vice versa.

=== BURN/BURP/BURW Identifier Vocabulary

The following quoins establish the minted-name vocabulary for the
BURN/BURP/BURW model.

[[burn_viceroyalty]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{burn_viceroyalty}::
The minted identifier of a
{burn_regime}
instance.
{burp_regime}
and
{burw_regime}
instances reference a node by its
{burn_viceroyalty}.
+
The
{burn_viceroyalty}
is conveyed by the directory containing the
{burn_regime}
assignment file;
it is not redundantly inscribed as a field within the file.
+
Examples: `redbox`, `winbox-prod`, `hawthorne`.

[[burp_investiture]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{burp_investiture}::
The minted identifier of a
{burp_regime}
instance — the operator's named grant of privileged authority over a
{burn_viceroyalty}.
+
The
{burp_investiture}
is conveyed by the directory containing the
{burp_regime}
assignment file;
it is not redundantly inscribed as a field within the file.
+
Examples: `redbox-bhyslop-admin`, `winbox-prod-alice-admin`.

[[burw_lieutenancy]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{burw_lieutenancy}::
The minted identifier of a
{burw_regime}
instance — a workload office operating on a
{burn_viceroyalty}
under the authority of a
{burp_investiture}.
A
{burw_lieutenancy}
names a workload entry (shell command, remote OS user, key file) that
materializes when a workload is conscripted.
+
The
{burw_lieutenancy}
is conveyed by the directory containing the
{burw_regime}
state file;
it is not redundantly inscribed as a field within the file.
+
The
{burw_lieutenancy}
string is by construction the remote OS username of its workload
account on the node, drawn at conscript time from the
{burn_viceroyalty}'s authoritative `burn_workloads` template.
+
Examples: `redbox-cyg`, `redbox-wsl`, `redbox-pwsh`.

[[burn_host_singularity]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{burn_host_singularity}::
The host of a node lives only in
{burn_regime}.
{burp_regime}
and
{burw_regime}
instances reach the host by reference
(via
{burp_node}
and
{burw_node}
respectively) and never duplicate it.
A given node has exactly one address-of-record;
credentials and workload state never re-state it.

[[bus_keys_operator_owned]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{bus_keys_operator_owned}::
The system never generates or modifies SSH key material.
The operator owns all key administration: keypair creation,
distribution of pubkeys into regime files, and placement of private
keys on the operator's station.
Regime files carry only the public-key line;
private keys live at operator-managed paths and are never written by
BUK procedures.

=== BURP Regime

[[burp_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burp_prefix}::
Bash Utility Regime Privileged

[[burp_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
{burp_regime}::
Per-station-user privileged-credential regime.
A
{burp_regime}
instance is identified by its
{burp_investiture}
(directory name).
+
{burp_regime}
references its node by
{burp_node}
per
{burn_host_singularity}.

[[burp_node]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burp_node}::
Reference to a
{burn_viceroyalty}
— the node this investiture is granted over.
Resolves to the
{burn_regime}
file at the directory bearing this name.

[[burp_user]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burp_user}::
Remote admin OS user this credential authenticates as
(e.g., `Administrator` on Windows, `root` or a sudo-capable user on
Linux/Mac).

[[burp_ssh_pubkey]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burp_ssh_pubkey}::
Full SSH public key line of the operator-managed admin keypair.
The matching private key lives at `${BURS_KEY_DIR}/<{burp_investiture}>` on
the operator's station per
{bus_keys_operator_owned}.

[[burp_command]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional axtu_string
{burp_command}::
Shell command for SSH `command=` routing in the node's
`administrators_authorized_keys`.
Empty for direct-shell connections;
non-empty when the admin shell is selected at the SSH layer
(e.g., `powershell.exe`, `C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe -l`).

=== BURW Regime

[[burw_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burw_prefix}::
Bash Utility Regime Workload

[[burw_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_file_sourced
{burw_regime}::
Per-station-user workload-state regime — a record of a workload office
materialized on a node by
{busn_conscript}.
A
{burw_regime}
instance is identified by its
{burw_lieutenancy}
(directory name).
Unlike
{burp_regime}
(operator-authored config),
{burw_regime}
is system-written by
{busn_conscript}
and consumed by
{busn_discharge},

1255	=== BURD Regime
1256	
1257	[[burd_prefix]]
1258	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
1259	{burd_prefix}::
1260	Bash Utility Dispatch Runtime
1261	
1262	[[burd_regime]]
1263	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
1264	{burd_regime}::
1265	Ephemeral runtime state assembled by bud_dispatch.sh during tabtarget execution.
1266	Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURD variables are constructed inline — no assignment file exists.
1267	The dispatch main function IS the kindle: zbud_setup sources configuration and derives computed state, zbud_process_args parses the tabtarget into tokens.
1268	
1269	==== Input Variables
1270	
1271	[[burd_group_input]]
1272	//axl_voices axhrgb_group
1273	{burd_group_input}::
1274	Variables set by the caller before dispatch invocation.
1275	
1276	[[burd_regime_file]]
1277	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1278	{burd_regime_file}::
1279	Path to the BURC regime assignment file to source during setup.
1280	
1281	[[burd_no_log]]
1282	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
1283	{burd_no_log}::
1284	When non-empty, suppresses all log file creation and output.
1285	
1286	[[burd_interactive]]
1287	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
1288	{burd_interactive}::
1289	When non-empty, enables interactive mode with uncurated tee logging.
1290	
1291	[[burd_coordinator_script]]
1292	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
1293	{burd_coordinator_script}::
1294	Path to the coordinator script (workbench/testbench) that handles the dispatched command.
1295	
1296	[[burd_launcher]]
1297	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
1298	{burd_launcher}::
1299	Path to the launcher bootstrap script, set by the tabtarget before exec.
1300	
1301	[[burd_station_file]]
1302	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
1303	{burd_station_file}::
1304	Absolute path to the developer's station regime assignment file.
1305	Set by the launcher from BURC_STATION_FILE.
1306	
1307	[[burd_term_cols]]
1308	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
1309	{burd_term_cols}::
1310	Terminal column width at dispatch time.
1311	Detected from stty if available, defaults to 80.
1312	
1313	==== Computed Variables
1314	
1315	[[burd_group_computed]]
1316	//axl_voices axhrgb_group
1317	{burd_group_computed}::
1318	Variables derived during dispatch setup.
1319	
1320	[[burd_now_stamp]]
1321	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1322	{burd_now_stamp}::
1323	Unique timestamp combining date, time, PID, and random component.
1324	
1325	[[burd_now_epoch]]
1326	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
1327	{burd_now_epoch}::
1328	UTC epoch seconds captured from same `date` invocation as {burd_now_stamp}.
1329	
1330	[[burd_temp_dir]]
1331	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1332	{burd_temp_dir}::
1333	Ephemeral directory for this dispatch invocation.
1334	
1335	[[burd_output_dir]]
1336	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1337	{burd_output_dir}::
1338	Fixed output directory cleared and recreated on each dispatch.
1339	
1340	[[burd_transcript]]
1341	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1342	{burd_transcript}::
1343	Path to the transcript file within the temporary directory.
1344	
1345	[[burd_git_context]]
1346	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1347	{burd_git_context}::
1348	Git describe output capturing repository state at dispatch time.
1349	
1350	[[burd_log_last]]
1351	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1352	{burd_log_last}::
1353	Path to the "last" log file, overwritten each dispatch.
1354	
1355	[[burd_log_same]]
1356	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1357	{burd_log_same}::
1358	Path to the curated "same" log file for the command+target combination.
1359	
1360	[[burd_log_hist]]
1361	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1362	{burd_log_hist}::
1363	Path to the timestamped historical log file.
1364	
1365	==== Parsed Variables
1366	
1367	[[burd_group_parsed]]
1368	//axl_voices axhrgb_group
1369	{burd_group_parsed}::
1370	Variables extracted from the tabtarget filename during argument processing.
1371	
1372	[[burd_command]]
1373	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1374	{burd_command}::
1375	Primary command token (first token from tabtarget split).
1376	
1377	[[burd_target]]
1378	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1379	{burd_target}::
1380	Complete tabtarget string as received by dispatch.

1383	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_repeated
1384	{burd_cli_args}::
1385	Array of additional CLI arguments passed after the tabtarget.
1386	
1387	[[burd_token_1]]
1388	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1389	{burd_token_1}::
1390	Positional token 1 from tabtarget split (the colophon).
1391	
1392	[[burd_token_2]]
1393	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1394	{burd_token_2}::
1395	Positional token 2 from tabtarget split (the frontispiece).
1396	
1397	[[burd_token_3]]
1398	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1399	{burd_token_3}::
1400	Positional token 3 from tabtarget split (the imprint, if present).
1401	
1402	[[burd_token_4]]
1403	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1404	{burd_token_4}::
1405	Positional token 4 from tabtarget split.
1406	
1407	[[burd_token_5]]
1408	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1409	{burd_token_5}::
1410	Positional token 5 from tabtarget split.
1411	
1412	=== BURE Regime
1413	
1414	[[bure_regime]]
1415	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
1416	{bure_regime}::
1417	Ambient regime for caller-supplied behavioral overrides to BUK primitives.
1418	Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURE variables are set directly in the caller's environment.
1419	Kindled and validated when buc_command.sh is sourced.
1420	
1421	[[bure_countdown]]
1422	//axvr_variable axtu_string axd_optional
1423	{bure_countdown}::
1424	Countdown behavior override.
1425	When set to `skip`, buc_countdown bypasses interactive countdown and tty output.
1426	When unset, countdown proceeds normally with interactive tty cancel window.
1427	Any other value fails validation.
1428	
1429	[[bure_verbose]]
1430	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
1431	{bure_verbose}::
1432	Verbosity level controlling diagnostic output.
1433	0=silent, 1=show messages, 2=enable bash trace.
1434	
1435	[[bure_color]]
1436	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1437	{bure_color}::
1438	Color output policy.
1439	Input as auto/0/1; resolved to 0 or 1 during dispatch.
1440	Respects NO_COLOR environment convention.
1441	
1442	==== Tweak Mechanism
1443	
1444	The tweak mechanism provides a single caller-supplied name/value override that flows through invocation without BUK interpreting its semantics.
1445	BUK enrolls, validates, and propagates the pair; consuming kits (e.g., RBK) check the name and act on the value.
1446	
1447	Constraint: one tweak per invocation.
1448	The caller sets at most one
1449	{bure_tweak_name}/{bure_tweak_value}
1450	pair in the environment before invoking a tabtarget.
1451	The pair is ambient — it propagates through `zbuto_invoke` like all BURE variables, without requiring re-kindle in the invoked command.
1452	
1453	[[bure_tweak_name]]
1454	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
1455	{bure_tweak_name}::
1456	Consumer-interpreted tweak identifier.
1457	The name selects which behavioral override the consuming kit applies.
1458	BUK validates length (0–64 characters) but assigns no meaning to the value.
1459	+
1460	Example: `graft_image` — tells RBK to substitute the graft source image.
1461	
1462	[[bure_tweak_value]]
1463	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
1464	{bure_tweak_value}::
1465	Consumer-interpreted tweak payload.
1466	The value is opaque to BUK; its format and interpretation are defined by whatever kit checks
1467	{bure_tweak_name}.
1468	BUK validates length (0–256 characters) but assigns no meaning to the value.
1469	+
1470	Either variable may be set independently — BUK does not enforce pairing.
1471	Consumer kits that require both must enforce that themselves.
1472	
1473	==== Exchange Labels
1474	
1475	[[bure_label]]
1476	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_xname axd_optional
1477	{bure_label}::
1478	Correlation label for cross-project exchange.
1479	When set, propagated verbatim into
1480	{burx_label}
1481	during dispatch.
1482	When unset, BUD writes empty string.
1483	Maximum 120 characters, xname format.
1484	+
1485	BUD writes whatever
1486	{bure_label}
1487	contains without interpreting the semantics.
1488	
1489	=== BURX Regime
1490	
1491	[[burx_prefix]]
1492	//axl_voices axrg_prefix
1493	{burx_prefix}::
1494	Bash Utility Exchange Regime
1495	
1496	[[burx_regime]]
1497	//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
1498	{burx_regime}::
1499	Exchange regime for cross-project dispatch state.
1500	Written by bud_dispatch.sh to both
1501	{burd_output_dir}
1502	and
1503	{burd_temp_dir}
1504	as `burx.env` via
1505	{buf_write_fact_single}.
1506	Unlike single-project regimes,
1507	{burx_regime}
1508	is designed to be read across a remote project boundary.
1509	+
1510	Variables appear in two phases:
1511	initial fields are written at dispatch start;
1512	terminal fields are appended at dispatch completion.
1513	Absence of
1514	{burx_exit_status}
1515	indicates the dispatch is still running.
1516	
1517	[[burx_pid]]
1518	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
1519	{burx_pid}::
1520	Process ID of the dispatched command.
1521	
1522	[[burx_began_at]]
1523	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1524	{burx_began_at}::
1525	Nanosecond-precision timestamp when dispatch started.
1526	Format: `YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.NNNNNNNNN`.
1527	
1528	[[burx_tabtarget]]
1529	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
1530	{burx_tabtarget}::
1531	The tabtarget that was dispatched.
1532	Corresponds to
1533	{burd_target}.
1534	
1535	[[burx_temp_dir]]
1536	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1537	{burx_temp_dir}::
1538	Absolute path to the dispatch temporary directory.
1539	Corresponds to
1540	{burd_temp_dir}.
1541	
1542	[[burx_transcript]]
1543	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1544	{burx_transcript}::
1545	Absolute path to the transcript file.
1546	Corresponds to
1547	{burd_transcript}.
1548	
1549	[[burx_log_hist]]
1550	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
1551	{burx_log_hist}::
1552	Absolute path to the historical log file.
1553	Corresponds to
1554	{burd_log_hist}.
1555	
1556	[[burx_label]]
1557	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
1558	{burx_label}::
1559	Correlation label from
1560	{bure_label}.
1561	Always present — empty string when
1562	{bure_label}
1563	is not set.
1564	Format and interpretation are opaque to BUD.
1565	
1566	[[burx_exit_status]]
1567	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer axd_optional
1568	{burx_exit_status}::
1569	Unix exit code of the dispatched command.
1570	Absence indicates the dispatch is still running.
1571	
1572	[[burx_ended_at]]
1573	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
1574	{burx_ended_at}::
1575	Nanosecond-precision timestamp when dispatch completed.
1576	Same format as
1577	{burx_began_at}.
1578	Present only when
1579	{burx_exit_status}
1580	is present.

1582	include::BUSD-DispatchRuntime.adoc[]
1583	
1584	== Remote Node Access
1585	
1586	Bringing remote nodes under SSH key control from a local station, establishing
1587	trust that supports both privileged node-level operations and ephemeral
1588	workload-tier work over the same {burn_regime}.
1589	
1590	=== Privilege Tiers
1591	
1592	The {burn_tier} field of every {burn_regime} profile classifies the profile
1593	as either `privileged` or `workload`. Tabtargets refuse aliases of the wrong
1594	tier at entry.
1595	
1596	==== Privileged Tier
1597	
1598	Privileged profiles authenticate as a remote OS user with admin or equivalent
1599	privileges. Used for node-level operations: sshd configuration, user
1600	provisioning, node-establishment ceremonies.
1601	
1602	Multiple station users may hold privileged trust on the same node (file
1603	format supports multi-resident keys). Operational convention: only one
1604	station user actively orchestrates work at a time. This convention is
1605	enforced by social contract, not BUK locking.
1606	
1607	==== Workload Tier
1608	
1609	Workload profiles authenticate as ephemeral remote OS users created by
1610	privileged authority. Used for normal remote work: running commands,
1611	file operations, interactive shells.
1612	
1613	Workload users are exclusive to one station user — not handed off between
1614	station users. Lifecycle is short:
1615	{busn_conscript}
1616	when needed,
1617	{busn_discharge}
1618	when done.
1619

[[burw_node]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burw_node}::
Reference to a

— the node this lieutenancy operates on.

=== BURD Regime

[[burd_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burd_prefix}::
Bash Utility Dispatch Runtime

[[burd_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
{burd_regime}::
Ephemeral runtime state assembled by bud_dispatch.sh during tabtarget execution.
Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURD variables are constructed inline — no assignment file exists.
The dispatch main function IS the kindle: zbud_setup sources configuration and derives computed state, zbud_process_args parses the tabtarget into tokens.

==== Input Variables

[[burd_group_input]]
//axl_voices axhrgb_group
{burd_group_input}::
Variables set by the caller before dispatch invocation.

[[burd_regime_file]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_regime_file}::
Path to the BURC regime assignment file to source during setup.

[[burd_no_log]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burd_no_log}::
When non-empty, suppresses all log file creation and output.

[[burd_interactive]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burd_interactive}::
When non-empty, enables interactive mode with uncurated tee logging.

[[burd_coordinator_script]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
{burd_coordinator_script}::
Path to the coordinator script (workbench/testbench) that handles the dispatched command.

[[burd_launcher]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
{burd_launcher}::
Path to the launcher bootstrap script, set by the tabtarget before exec.

[[burd_station_file]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path axd_required
{burd_station_file}::
Absolute path to the developer's station regime assignment file.
Set by the launcher from BURC_STATION_FILE.

[[burd_term_cols]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{burd_term_cols}::
Terminal column width at dispatch time.
Detected from stty if available, defaults to 80.

==== Computed Variables

[[burd_group_computed]]
//axl_voices axhrgb_group
{burd_group_computed}::
Variables derived during dispatch setup.

[[burd_now_stamp]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_now_stamp}::
Unique timestamp combining date, time, PID, and random component.

[[burd_now_epoch]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{burd_now_epoch}::
UTC epoch seconds captured from same `date` invocation as {burd_now_stamp}.

[[burd_temp_dir]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_temp_dir}::
Ephemeral directory for this dispatch invocation.

[[burd_output_dir]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_output_dir}::
Fixed output directory cleared and recreated on each dispatch.

[[burd_transcript]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_transcript}::
Path to the transcript file within the temporary directory.

[[burd_git_context]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_git_context}::
Git describe output capturing repository state at dispatch time.

[[burd_log_last]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_log_last}::
Path to the "last" log file, overwritten each dispatch.

[[burd_log_same]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_log_same}::
Path to the curated "same" log file for the command+target combination.

[[burd_log_hist]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burd_log_hist}::
Path to the timestamped historical log file.

==== Parsed Variables

[[burd_group_parsed]]
//axl_voices axhrgb_group
{burd_group_parsed}::
Variables extracted from the tabtarget filename during argument processing.

[[burd_command]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_command}::
Primary command token (first token from tabtarget split).

[[burd_target]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_target}::
Complete tabtarget string as received by dispatch.

[[burd_cli_args]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_repeated
{burd_cli_args}::
Array of additional CLI arguments passed after the tabtarget.

[[burd_token_1]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_token_1}::
Positional token 1 from tabtarget split (the colophon).

[[burd_token_2]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_token_2}::
Positional token 2 from tabtarget split (the frontispiece).

[[burd_token_3]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_token_3}::
Positional token 3 from tabtarget split (the imprint, if present).

[[burd_token_4]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_token_4}::
Positional token 4 from tabtarget split.

[[burd_token_5]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burd_token_5}::
Positional token 5 from tabtarget split.

=== BURE Regime

[[bure_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
{bure_regime}::
Ambient regime for caller-supplied behavioral overrides to BUK primitives.
Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURE variables are set directly in the caller's environment.
Kindled and validated when buc_command.sh is sourced.

[[bure_countdown]]
//axvr_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{bure_countdown}::
Countdown behavior override.
When set to `skip`, buc_countdown bypasses interactive countdown and tty output.
When unset, countdown proceeds normally with interactive tty cancel window.
Any other value fails validation.

[[bure_verbose]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{bure_verbose}::
Verbosity level controlling diagnostic output.
0=silent, 1=show messages, 2=enable bash trace.

[[bure_color]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{bure_color}::
Color output policy.
Input as auto/0/1; resolved to 0 or 1 during dispatch.
Respects NO_COLOR environment convention.

==== Tweak Mechanism

The tweak mechanism provides a single caller-supplied name/value override that flows through invocation without BUK interpreting its semantics.
BUK enrolls, validates, and propagates the pair; consuming kits (e.g., RBK) check the name and act on the value.

Constraint: one tweak per invocation.
The caller sets at most one
{bure_tweak_name}/{bure_tweak_value}
pair in the environment before invoking a tabtarget.
The pair is ambient — it propagates through `zbuto_invoke` like all BURE variables, without requiring re-kindle in the invoked command.

[[bure_tweak_name]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{bure_tweak_name}::
Consumer-interpreted tweak identifier.
The name selects which behavioral override the consuming kit applies.
BUK validates length (0–64 characters) but assigns no meaning to the value.
+
Example: `graft_image` — tells RBK to substitute the graft source image.

[[bure_tweak_value]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{bure_tweak_value}::
Consumer-interpreted tweak payload.
The value is opaque to BUK; its format and interpretation are defined by whatever kit checks
{bure_tweak_name}.
BUK validates length (0–256 characters) but assigns no meaning to the value.
+
Either variable may be set independently — BUK does not enforce pairing.
Consumer kits that require both must enforce that themselves.

==== Exchange Labels

[[bure_label]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_xname axd_optional
{bure_label}::
Correlation label for cross-project exchange.
When set, propagated verbatim into
{burx_label}
during dispatch.
When unset, BUD writes empty string.
Maximum 120 characters, xname format.
+
BUD writes whatever
{bure_label}
contains without interpreting the semantics.

=== BURX Regime

[[burx_prefix]]
//axl_voices axrg_prefix
{burx_prefix}::
Bash Utility Exchange Regime

[[burx_regime]]
//axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton
{burx_regime}::
Exchange regime for cross-project dispatch state.
Written by bud_dispatch.sh to both
{burd_output_dir}
and
{burd_temp_dir}
as `burx.env` via
{buf_write_fact_single}.
Unlike single-project regimes,
{burx_regime}
is designed to be read across a remote project boundary.
+
Variables appear in two phases:
initial fields are written at dispatch start;
terminal fields are appended at dispatch completion.
Absence of
{burx_exit_status}
indicates the dispatch is still running.

[[burx_pid]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{burx_pid}::
Process ID of the dispatched command.

[[burx_began_at]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burx_began_at}::
Nanosecond-precision timestamp when dispatch started.
Format: `YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.NNNNNNNNN`.

[[burx_tabtarget]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{burx_tabtarget}::
The tabtarget that was dispatched.
Corresponds to
{burd_target}.

[[burx_temp_dir]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burx_temp_dir}::
Absolute path to the dispatch temporary directory.
Corresponds to
{burd_temp_dir}.

[[burx_transcript]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burx_transcript}::
Absolute path to the transcript file.
Corresponds to
{burd_transcript}.

[[burx_log_hist]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{burx_log_hist}::
Absolute path to the historical log file.
Corresponds to
{burd_log_hist}.

[[burx_label]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burx_label}::
Correlation label from
{bure_label}.
Always present — empty string when
{bure_label}
is not set.
Format and interpretation are opaque to BUD.

[[burx_exit_status]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer axd_optional
{burx_exit_status}::
Unix exit code of the dispatched command.
Absence indicates the dispatch is still running.

[[burx_ended_at]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string axd_optional
{burx_ended_at}::
Nanosecond-precision timestamp when dispatch completed.
Same format as
{burx_began_at}.
Present only when
{burx_exit_status}
is present.

include::BUSD-DispatchRuntime.adoc[]

== Remote Node Access

Bringing remote nodes under SSH key control from a local station, establishing
trust that supports both privileged node-level operations and ephemeral
workload-tier work over the same {burn_regime}.

=== Privilege Tiers

The {burn_tier} field of every {burn_regime} profile classifies the profile
as either `privileged` or `workload`. Tabtargets refuse aliases of the wrong
tier at entry.

==== Privileged Tier

Privileged profiles authenticate as a remote OS user with admin or equivalent
privileges. Used for node-level operations: sshd configuration, user
provisioning, node-establishment ceremonies.

Multiple station users may hold privileged trust on the same node (file
format supports multi-resident keys). Operational convention: only one
station user actively orchestrates work at a time. This convention is
enforced by social contract, not BUK locking.

==== Workload Tier

Workload profiles authenticate as ephemeral remote OS users created by
privileged authority. Used for normal remote work: running commands,
file operations, interactive shells.

Workload users are exclusive to one station user — not handed off between
station users. Lifecycle is short:
{busn_conscript}
when needed,
{busn_discharge}
when done.

=== Tabtarget Catalog

Tabtargets under `buw-` consume {burn_regime} profiles to perform remote
operations. Tier-specific colophons follow `buw-n[pw]` for clarity.

==== Lifecycle

[[bust_node_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_list}::
`buw-rnl` —
List all {burn_regime} profiles in the local station subtree.

[[bust_node_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_render}::
`buw-rnr` —
Render a single {burn_regime} profile (alias passed as positional argument).

[[bust_node_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_validate}::
`buw-rnv` —
Validate a single {burn_regime} profile (alias passed as positional argument).

==== Privileged (per-platform node setup)

[[bust_garrison_linux]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_linux}::
`buw-npgl` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for Linux nodes.

[[bust_garrison_mac]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_mac}::
`buw-npgm` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for macOS nodes.

[[bust_garrison_cygwin]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_cygwin}::
`buw-npgc` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for Cygwin nodes.

[[bust_garrison_wsl]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_wsl}::
`buw-npgw` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for WSL nodes.

[[bust_garrison_powershell]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_powershell}::
`buw-npgp` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for PowerShell nodes.

[[bust_garrison_localhost]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_localhost}::
`buw-npgx` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for localhost nodes.

==== Workload (privileged authority required)

[[bust_conscript_linux]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_linux}::
`buw-nwcl` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a Linux shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_mac]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_mac}::
`buw-nwcm` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a macOS shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_cygwin]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_cygwin}::
`buw-nwcc` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a Cygwin shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_wsl]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_wsl}::
`buw-nwcw` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a WSL shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_powershell]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_powershell}::
`buw-nwcp` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a PowerShell shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_conscript_localhost]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_conscript_localhost}::
`buw-nwcx` —
{busn_conscript} a workload user with a localhost shell, via the named privileged BURN.

[[bust_discharge]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_discharge}::
`buw-nwd` —
{busn_discharge} a workload user.
Deletes the remote OS user via the workload profile's privileged
authority and removes the local profile.

[[bust_inventory]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_inventory}::
`buw-nwi` —
{busn_inventory} workload users present on the node, queried via the
privileged BURN.

==== Workload (operational)

[[bust_knock]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_knock}::
`buw-nwk` —
Probe workload responsiveness via passwordless SSH.

[[bust_remote_run]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_remote_run}::
`buw-nwr` —
Run the contents of a command file on the remote node; capture output to
{burd_output_dir}.

[[bust_interactive_session]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_interactive_session}::
`buw-nws` —
Open an interactive SSH session to the workload account.

==== Handbook

[[bust_handbook_node_top]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_handbook_node_top}::
`buw-hn0` —
Top-level handbook landing for node access procedures.

=== Privileges

The {burs_user} owns its profile subtree at `.buk/users/{burs_user}/`
exclusively — operations write only under the current station user's tree.

A {burn_regime} profile describes a connection identity (host, user, shell,
alias, key, tier) — not project state, not work-directory. Project context
is supplied by the consumer at invocation time.

Each platform subdoc declares the remote-user privilege contract that its
ceremony assumes (e.g., on Windows the privileged user must be a member of
the Administrators group).

=== Verb Definitions

[[busn_garrison]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_garrison}::
Establish privileged BURN trust on a node by writing the privileged
{burn_regime} profile and running the platform-specific ceremony to gain
admin access. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-npg{platform}`. First-time
node setup; idempotent on re-run.

[[busn_conscript]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_conscript}::
Create an ephemeral workload user on a node and provision a workload
{burn_regime} profile locally. Requires a privileged BURN as authority for
the remote-user creation. Per-platform tabtargets at `buw-nwc{platform}`,
where the platform letter selects the workload shell — independent of the
privileged BURN's shell choice.

[[busn_discharge]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_discharge}::
Remove a workload user from a node and delete the local workload
{burn_regime} profile. The workload profile encodes its privileged
authority; {busn_discharge} uses that authority to delete the remote OS
user. Tabtarget: `buw-nwd`.

[[busn_inventory]]
//axl_voices axt_command
{busn_inventory}::
List workload users currently provisioned on a node, queried via a
privileged BURN. Scope is the node's full workload-user population — not
just the current station user's history. Tabtarget: `buw-nwi`.

=== Input Artifacts

[[busi_scribed_command_file]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busi_scribed_command_file}::
A file authored by the operator containing commands to be executed
on the remote node by {bust_remote_run}.
Path passed as the second positional argument; contents are streamed
to the remote shell at dispatch time.

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BURS Regime - Bash Utility Regime Station Module

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZBURS_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module burs multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZBURS_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zburs_*)

zburs_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBURS_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module burs already kindled"

  # No defaults set — buv uses ${!varname:-} for safe indirect expansion under set -u.
  # Unset variables are detected distinctly from empty by zbuv_check_capture.

  # Enroll all BURS variables — single source of truth for validation and rendering

  buv_regime_enroll BURS

  buv_group_enroll "Developer Identity"
  buv_xname_enroll   BURS_USER     1   32  "Local developer username (routes to .buk/users/ profiles)"

  buv_group_enroll "Developer Logging"
  buv_string_enroll  BURS_LOG_DIR  1  512  "Directory for BUK operation logs"

  # Guard against unexpected BURS_ variables not in enrollment
  buv_scope_sentinel BURS BURS_

  # Lock all enrolled BURS_ variables against mutation
  buv_lock BURS

  readonly ZBURS_KINDLED=1
}

zburs_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURS_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burs not kindled - call zburs_kindle first"
}

# Enforce all BURS enrollment validations
zburs_enforce() {
  zburs_sentinel

  buv_vet BURS
}

# eof

#!/bin/bash
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Bash Validation Utility Library
# Compatible with Bash 3.2 (e.g., macOS default shell)

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZBUV_INCLUDED:-}" || return 0
ZBUV_INCLUDED=1

# Literal constants — check capture output protocol markers
BUV_check_gated="gated"
BUV_check_fail="fail:"

# Source the console utility library
ZBUV_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
source "${ZBUV_SCRIPT_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

buv_file_exists() {
  local z_filepath="${1:-}"
  test -f "${z_filepath}" || buc_die "Required file not found: ${z_filepath}"
}

buv_dir_exists() {
  local z_dirpath="${1:-}"
  test -d "${z_dirpath}" || buc_die "Required directory not found: ${z_dirpath}"
}

buv_dir_empty() {
  local z_dirpath="${1:-}"
  test -d "${z_dirpath}" || buc_die "Required directory not found: ${z_dirpath}"
  local z_check_file
  z_check_file=$(mktemp)
  find "${z_dirpath}" -maxdepth 1 -mindepth 1 -print -quit > "${z_check_file}"
  test ! -s "${z_check_file}" || { rm -f "${z_check_file}"; buc_die "Directory must be empty: ${z_dirpath}"; }
  rm -f "${z_check_file}"
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enrollment infrastructure
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

zbuv_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZBUV_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module buv already kindled"

  # Enrollment rolls (9 parallel arrays)
  z_buv_scope_roll=()
  z_buv_varname_roll=()
  z_buv_type_roll=()
  z_buv_gate_var_roll=()
  z_buv_gate_val_roll=()
  z_buv_p1_roll=()
  z_buv_p2_roll=()
  z_buv_group_roll=()
  z_buv_desc_roll=()

  # Group registry rolls (4 parallel arrays, foreign-keyed by group title)
  z_buv_grp_scope_roll=()
  z_buv_grp_title_roll=()
  z_buv_grp_gate_var_roll=()
  z_buv_grp_gate_val_roll=()

  # Mutable kindle state: regime context (set by buv_regime_enroll / buv_group_enroll / buv_gate_enroll)
  z_buv_current_scope=""
  z_buv_current_group=""
  z_buv_current_group_idx=-1
  z_buv_current_gate_var=""
  z_buv_current_gate_val=""

  readonly ZBUV_KINDLED=1
}

zbuv_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBUV_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module buv not kindled - call zbuv_kindle first"
}

# Test support: reset enrollment state without re-kindling
zbuv_reset_enrollment() {
  zbuv_sentinel

  # Clear enrollment rolls
  z_buv_scope_roll=()
  z_buv_varname_roll=()
  z_buv_type_roll=()
  z_buv_gate_var_roll=()
  z_buv_gate_val_roll=()

content.subject: Survey BUS0 spec and BUSTLL/LR/LV subdocs
content.description: Read BUS0 spec to understand current state of BURN section, mapping section, quoin patterns. Read BUSTLL/LR/LV subdocs to learn the mirror pattern for BUSTPL/PR/PV.

content.subject: Update BUS0 spec for BURN reshape and BURP mint
content.description: Drop deprecated BURN field mapping entries + body anchors. Add BURN_PLATFORM mapping entry + body. Mint BURP regime mapping section + field bodies. Mint bust_privilege_regime_{list,render,validate} quoins + bodies. Add three new BUSTPx include lines.

content.subject: Reshape burn_regime.sh and burn_cli.sh
content.description: Drop BURN_TIER, BURN_USER, BURN_ALIAS, BURN_SSH_PUBKEY, BURN_KEY_FILE, BURN_COMMAND. Add BURN_PLATFORM enum (linux/mac/cygwin/wsl/powershell/localhost). Final field set: BURN_HOST + BURN_PLATFORM. Update path from .buk/users/${BURS_USER}/<alias>/burn.env to .buk/rbmn_nodes/<viceroyalty>/burn.env. Add friendly-error UX for missing folio.

content.subject: Mint burp_regime.sh, burp_cli.sh, burp.env template
content.description: Mirror BURN N-instance pattern. Fields: BURP_NODE, BURP_USER, BURP_SSH_PUBKEY, BURP_KEY_FILE, optional BURP_COMMAND. Path: .buk/rbmu_users/<user>/<investiture>/burp.env. Friendly-error UX for missing folio.

content.subject: Mint buw-rp{l,r,v} tabtargets and BUSTPx subdocs
content.description: Tabtargets buw-rpl/rpr/rpv mirror buw-rnl/rnr/rnv. Subdocs BUSTPL/PR/PV mirror BUSTLL/LR/LV.

content.subject: Migrate storage layout and delete legacy burn.env files
content.description: Rename .buk/users/ to .buk/rbmu_users/. Delete the 7 existing burn.env files (operator reauthors). Verify zipper / workbench enrollment still resolves.

content.subject: Validate: BUS0 asciidoc clean, tabtargets work
content.description: Done-when checks: tt/buw-rn{l,r,v} read from rbmn_nodes/, tt/buw-rp{l,r,v} read from rbmu_users/, BURP profile authorable by hand and validates clean, friendly-error messages land for both regimes, BUS0 validates (asciidoc clean, no orphaned cross-references).

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUK Workbench - Routes BUK management commands via zipper registry
#
# All commands dispatch via buz_exec_lookup (see buwz_zipper.sh for colophon mapping).

set -euo pipefail

# Get script directory
BUW_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"

# Source dependencies
source "${BUW_SCRIPT_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
source "${BUW_SCRIPT_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
source "${BUW_SCRIPT_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
source "${BUW_SCRIPT_DIR}/buwz_zipper.sh"

# Show filename on each displayed line
buc_context "${0##*/}"

# Kindle dispatch and zipper registry
zbuv_kindle
zburd_kindle
zbuz_kindle
zbuwz_kindle

# Verbose output if BURE_VERBOSE is set
buw_show() {
  test "${BURE_VERBOSE:-0}" != "1" || echo "BUWSHOW: $*"
}

######################################################################
# Routing

buw_route() {
  local z_command="$1"
  shift

  buw_show "Routing command: ${z_command} with args: $*"

  zburd_sentinel
  zbuwz_healthcheck

  buw_show "BURD environment verified"

  buz_exec_lookup "${z_command}" "${BUW_SCRIPT_DIR}" "$@"
}

buw_main() {
  local z_command="${1:-}"
  shift || true

  test -n "${z_command}" || buc_die "No command specified"

  buw_route "${z_command}" "$@"
}

buw_main "$@"

# eof

z_buv_p1_roll+=("${z_p1}")
  z_buv_p2_roll+=("${z_p2}")
  z_buv_group_roll+=("${z_buv_current_group}")
  z_buv_desc_roll+=("${z_desc}")
}

# Public enrollment functions — scalar types
#
# Signature: buv_TYPE_enroll VARNAME P1 P2 "description"
# Scope from buv_regime_enroll; group from buv_group_enroll; gate from buv_gate_enroll.

buv_string_enroll() {
  local z_varname="${1:-}"
  local z_p1="${2:-}"
  local z_p2="${3:-}"
  local z_desc="${4:-}"
  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "string" "${z_p1}" "${z_p2}" "${z_desc}"
}

buv_secret_enroll() {
  local z_varname="${1:-}"
  local z_p1="${2:-}"
  local z_p2="${3:-}"
  local z_desc="${4:-}"
  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "secret" "${z_p1}" "${z_p2}" "${z_desc}"
}

buv_xname_enroll() {
  local z_varname="${1:-}"
  local z_p1="${2:-}"
  local z_p2="${3:-}"
  local z_desc="${4:-}"
  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "xname" "${z_p1}" "${z_p2}" "${z_desc}"
}

buv_gname_enroll() {
  local z_varname="${1:-}"
  local z_p1="${2:-}"
  local z_p2="${3:-}"
  local z_desc="${4:-}"
  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "gname" "${z_p1}" "${z_p2}" "${z_desc}"
}

buv_fqin_enroll() {
  local z_varname="${1:-}"
  local z_p1="${2:-}"
  local z_p2="${3:-}"
  local z_desc="${4:-}"
  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "fqin" "${z_p1}" "${z_p2}" "${z_desc}"
}

buv_bool_enroll() {
  local z_varname="${1:-}"
  local z_desc="${2:-}"
  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "bool" "" "" "${z_desc}"
}

buv_enum_enroll() {
  local z_varname="${1:-}"
  local z_desc="${2:-}"
  shift 2
  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "enum" "$*" "" "${z_desc}"
}

buv_decimal_enroll() {
  local z_varname="${1:-}"
  local z_p1="${2:-}"
  local z_p2="${3:-}"
  local z_desc="${4:-}"
  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "decimal" "${z_p1}" "${z_p2}" "${z_desc}"
}

buv_odref_enroll() {
  local z_varname="${1:-}"
  local z_desc="${2:-}"
  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "odref" "" "" "${z_desc}"
}

buv_ipv4_enroll() {
  local z_varname="${1:-}"
  local z_desc="${2:-}"
  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "ipv4" "" "" "${z_desc}"
}

buv_port_enroll() {
  local z_varname="${1:-}"
  local z_desc="${2:-}"
  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "port" "" "" "${z_desc}"
}

# Public enrollment functions — list types

buv_list_string_enroll() {
  local z_varname="${1:-}"
  local z_p1="${2:-}"
  local z_p2="${3:-}"
  local z_desc="${4:-}"
  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "list_string" "${z_p1}" "${z_p2}" "${z_desc}"
}

buv_list_ipv4_enroll() {
  local z_varname="${1:-}"
  local z_desc="${2:-}"
  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "list_ipv4" "" "" "${z_desc}"
}

buv_list_gname_enroll() {
  local z_varname="${1:-}"
  local z_p1="${2:-}"
  local z_p2="${3:-}"
  local z_desc="${4:-}"
  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "list_gname" "${z_p1}" "${z_p2}" "${z_desc}"
}

buv_list_cidr_enroll() {
  local z_varname="${1:-}"
  local z_desc="${2:-}"
  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "list_cidr" "" "" "${z_desc}"
}

buv_list_domain_enroll() {
  local z_varname="${1:-}"
  local z_desc="${2:-}"
  zbuv_enroll "${z_varname}" "list_domain" "" "" "${z_desc}"
}

# Internal check capture — validates a single enrolled variable by roll index.
# Returns error detail on stdout (empty = pass).
# Echo "${BUV_check_gated}" when gate doesn't match (caller decides skip behavior).
# Echo "${BUV_check_fail}<detail>" on validation failure.

content.subject: Survey BUS0 spec and BUSTLL/LR/LV subdocs
content.description: Read BUS0 spec to understand current state of BURN section, mapping section, quoin patterns. Read BUSTLL/LR/LV subdocs to learn the mirror pattern for BUSTPL/PR/PV.

content.subject: Update BUS0 spec for BURN reshape and BURP mint
content.description: Drop deprecated BURN field mapping entries + body anchors. Add BURN_PLATFORM mapping entry + body. Mint BURP regime mapping section + field bodies. Mint bust_privilege_regime_{list,render,validate} quoins + bodies. Add three new BUSTPx include lines.

content.subject: Reshape burn_regime.sh and burn_cli.sh
content.description: Drop BURN_TIER, BURN_USER, BURN_ALIAS, BURN_SSH_PUBKEY, BURN_KEY_FILE, BURN_COMMAND. Add BURN_PLATFORM enum (linux/mac/cygwin/wsl/powershell/localhost). Final field set: BURN_HOST + BURN_PLATFORM. Update path from .buk/users/${BURS_USER}/<alias>/burn.env to .buk/rbmn_nodes/<viceroyalty>/burn.env. Add friendly-error UX for missing folio.

content.subject: Mint burp_regime.sh, burp_cli.sh, burp.env template
content.description: Mirror BURN N-instance pattern. Fields: BURP_NODE, BURP_USER, BURP_SSH_PUBKEY, BURP_KEY_FILE, optional BURP_COMMAND. Path: .buk/rbmu_users/<user>/<investiture>/burp.env. Friendly-error UX for missing folio.

content.subject: Mint buw-rp{l,r,v} tabtargets and BUSTPx subdocs
content.description: Tabtargets buw-rpl/rpr/rpv mirror buw-rnl/rnr/rnv. Subdocs BUSTPL/PR/PV mirror BUSTLL/LR/LV.

content.subject: Migrate storage layout and delete legacy burn.env files
content.description: Rename .buk/users/ to .buk/rbmu_users/. Delete the 7 existing burn.env files (operator reauthors). Verify zipper / workbench enrollment still resolves.

content.subject: Validate: BUS0 asciidoc clean, tabtargets work
content.description: Done-when checks: tt/buw-rn{l,r,v} read from rbmn_nodes/, tt/buw-rp{l,r,v} read from rbmu_users/, BURP profile authorable by hand and validates clean, friendly-error messages land for both regimes, BUS0 validates (asciidoc clean, no orphaned cross-references).

12

file.content:
# BUK Users Directory

Per-user configuration profiles, keyed by `BURS_USER` from the station regime.

## Structure

```
.buk/users/<BURS_USER>/<alias>/burn.env
```

Each subdirectory under a user is a BURN node regime profile — one SSH connection configuration. The `<alias>` must match the `BURN_ALIAS` value inside the `burn.env` file.

`BURN_KEY_FILE` specifies the SSH private key filename in `~/.ssh/`. When empty, defaults to the alias name. Localhost profiles set `id_ed25519` to share the invoking user's default key.

## Git Safety

BURN profiles contain **public** key material only (`BURN_SSH_PUBKEY`). Private keys are never stored here. These files are safe to commit.

## Example

```
.buk/users/bhyslop/winhost-cyg/burn.env   # SSH to Windows via Cygwin bash
.buk/users/bhyslop/winhost-wsl/burn.env   # SSH to Windows via WSL
.buk/users/bhyslop/winhost-ps/burn.env    # SSH to Windows via PowerShell
```

## Validation

```bash
tt/buw-rhva.ValidateAllHostProfiles.sh     # all profiles for current BURS_USER
tt/buw-rhv.ValidateHostProfile.sh <alias>  # single profile
```

input.old_string:
[[burn_host]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burn_host}::
IP address or hostname of the remote node.

[[burn_os_family]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burn_os_family}::
Operating-system family of the node: `linux`, `mac`, or `windows`.
Drives platform-specific procedure dispatch (OS-user creation
mechanism, file-permission tooling, service-restart command).

[[burn_workloads]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string axd_repeated
{burn_workloads}::
Authoritative template of valid
{burw_lieutenancy}
strings on this node.
{busn_conscript}
may only materialize a workload whose alias is enumerated here;
the operator pre-declares the node's supported workload offices.

[[burn_user]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burn_user}::
Username on the remote node.
May differ from the local
{burs_user}.

[[burn_alias]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burn_alias}::
SSH alias and key filename.
Must match the directory name containing this profile's assignment file.

[[burn_ssh_pubkey]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burn_ssh_pubkey}::
Full SSH public key line (e.g., `ssh-ed25519 AAAA... user@host`).
Git-transported: no private key material.
Used by handbook procedures to render `authorized_keys` entries and `ssh-keygen` commands.

[[burn_key_file]]
//axvr_variable axd_optional axtu_string
{burn_key_file}::
SSH private key filename within `~/.ssh/`.
Empty defaults to {burn_alias} (one key per connection).
Localhost profiles set `id_ed25519` to share the invoking user's default key.

[[burn_command]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_string
{burn_command}::
Shell command for `command=` routing in SSH `authorized_keys`.
Empty for direct-shell connections (Linux, macOS, localhost — no routing needed).
When set, determines which shell or environment the connection launches
(e.g., `C:\cygwin64\bin\bash.exe -l` for Cygwin, `wsl.exe -d Ubuntu` for WSL).

[[burn_tier]]
//axvr_variable axd_required axtu_xname
{burn_tier}::
Privilege classification of this profile.
Values: `privileged` (admin-level operations on the node — sshd configuration,
user provisioning, node-level setup) or `workload` (normal remote work as an
ephemeral user on an established node).
Tabtargets enforce tier preconditions at entry: privileged-only operations
refuse workload aliases and vice versa.

station users. Lifecycle is short:
{busn_conscript}
when needed,
{busn_discharge}
when done.

=== Tabtarget Catalog

Tabtargets under `buw-` consume {burn_regime} profiles to perform remote
operations. Tier-specific colophons follow `buw-n[pw]` for clarity.

==== Lifecycle

[[bust_node_regime_list]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_list}::
`buw-rnl` —
List all {burn_regime} profiles in the local station subtree.

[[bust_node_regime_render]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_render}::
`buw-rnr` —
Render a single {burn_regime} profile (alias passed as positional argument).

[[bust_node_regime_validate]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_node_regime_validate}::
`buw-rnv` —
Validate a single {burn_regime} profile (alias passed as positional argument).

==== Privileged (per-platform node setup)

[[bust_garrison_linux]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_linux}::
`buw-npgl` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for Linux nodes.

[[bust_garrison_mac]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_mac}::
`buw-npgm` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for macOS nodes.

[[bust_garrison_cygwin]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_cygwin}::
`buw-npgc` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for Cygwin nodes.

[[bust_garrison_wsl]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_wsl}::
`buw-npgw` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for WSL nodes.

[[bust_garrison_powershell]]
//axvo_tabtarget
{bust_garrison_powershell}::
`buw-npgp` —
{busn_garrison} ceremony for PowerShell nodes.

[[bust_garrison_localhost]]

==== Workload (privileged authority required)

content.content:
1530	{burx_ended_at}::
1531	Nanosecond-precision timestamp when dispatch completed.
1532	Same format as
1533	{burx_began_at}.
1534	Present only when
1535	{burx_exit_status}
1536	is present.
1537	
1538	include::BUSD-DispatchRuntime.adoc[]
1539	
1540	== Remote Node Access
1541	
1542	Bringing remote nodes under SSH key control from a local station, establishing
1543	trust that supports both privileged node-level operations and ephemeral
1544	workload-tier work over the same {burn_regime}.
1545	
1546	=== Privilege Tiers
1547	
1548	The {burn_tier} field of every {burn_regime} profile classifies the profile
1549	as either `privileged` or `workload`. Tabtargets refuse aliases of the wrong
1550	tier at entry.
1551	
1552	==== Privileged Tier
1553	
1554	Privileged profiles authenticate as a remote OS user with admin or equivalent
1555	privileges. Used for node-level operations: sshd configuration, user
1556	provisioning, node-establishment ceremonies.
1557	
1558	Multiple station users may hold privileged trust on the same node (file
1559	format supports multi-resident keys). Operational convention: only one
1560	station user actively orchestrates work at a time. This convention is
1561	enforced by social contract, not BUK locking.
1562	
1563	==== Workload Tier
1564	
1565	Workload profiles authenticate as ephemeral remote OS users created by
1566	privileged authority. Used for normal remote work: running commands,
1567	file operations, interactive shells.
1568	
1569	Workload users are exclusive to one station user — not handed off between
1570	station users. Lifecycle is short:
1571	{busn_conscript}
1572	when needed,
1573	{busn_discharge}
1574	when done.
1575	
1576	=== Tabtarget Catalog
1577	
1578	Tabtargets under `buw-` consume {burn_regime} profiles to perform remote
1579	operations. Tier-specific colophons follow `buw-n[pw]` for clarity.

file.content:
{burx_ended_at}::
Nanosecond-precision timestamp when dispatch completed.
Same format as
{burx_began_at}.
Present only when
{burx_exit_status}
is present.

include::BUSD-DispatchRuntime.adoc[]

== Remote Node Access

Bringing remote nodes under SSH key control from a local station, establishing
trust that supports both privileged node-level operations and ephemeral
workload-tier work over the same {burn_regime}.

=== Privilege Tiers

The {burn_tier} field of every {burn_regime} profile classifies the profile
as either `privileged` or `workload`. Tabtargets refuse aliases of the wrong
tier at entry.

==== Privileged Tier

Privileged profiles authenticate as a remote OS user with admin or equivalent
privileges. Used for node-level operations: sshd configuration, user
provisioning, node-establishment ceremonies.

Multiple station users may hold privileged trust on the same node (file
format supports multi-resident keys). Operational convention: only one
station user actively orchestrates work at a time. This convention is
enforced by social contract, not BUK locking.

==== Workload Tier

Workload profiles authenticate as ephemeral remote OS users created by
privileged authority. Used for normal remote work: running commands,
file operations, interactive shells.

Workload users are exclusive to one station user — not handed off between
station users. Lifecycle is short:
{busn_conscript}
when needed,
{busn_discharge}
when done.

=== Tabtarget Catalog

Tabtargets under `buw-` consume {burn_regime} profiles to perform remote
operations. Tier-specific colophons follow `buw-n[pw]` for clarity.

file.content:
{bust_handbook_node_top}::
`buw-hn0` —
Top-level handbook landing for node access procedures.

=== Privileges

The {burs_user} owns its profile subtree at `.buk/users/{burs_user}/`
exclusively — operations write only under the current station user's tree.

A {burn_regime} profile describes a connection identity (host, user, shell,
alias, key, tier) — not project state, not work-directory. Project context
is supplied by the consumer at invocation time.

Each platform subdoc declares the remote-user privilege contract that its
ceremony assumes (e.g., on Windows the privileged user must be a member of
the Administrators group).

=== Verb Definitions

[[busn_garrison]]

input.old_string:
=== Privileges

The {burs_user} owns its profile subtree at `.buk/users/{burs_user}/`
exclusively — operations write only under the current station user's tree.

A {burn_regime} profile describes a connection identity (host, user, shell,
alias, key, tier) — not project state, not work-directory. Project context
is supplied by the consumer at invocation time.

Each platform subdoc declares the remote-user privilege contract that its
ceremony assumes (e.g., on Windows the privileged user must be a member of
the Administrators group).
input.new_string:
=== Privileges

A
{burn_regime}
instance lives under `.buk/rbmn_nodes/<viceroyalty>/` and is shared
across station users (git-transported); it carries node shape only and
no key material.
A
{burp_regime}
instance lives under `.buk/rbmu_users/<burs_user>/<investiture>/`;
the
{burs_user}
owns its privileged-credential subtree exclusively, and operations
write only under the current station user's tree.

Each platform subdoc declares the remote-user privilege contract that its
ceremony assumes (e.g., on Windows the privileged user must be a member of
the Administrators group).

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
